programme.title: The Warrior
programme.desc: A warlord's warrior in ancient India who is sent to massacre a village behind in their taxes suffers a crisis of conscience and, laying down his sword, sets off to the Himalayas in search of peace. The vengeful warlord sends enforcers on the trail of his renegade warrior and he must once again face violence.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Frogmen
programme.desc: Powerful and engrossing World War II tale centred on the exploits of frogmen in an underwater demolition team, headed by Lieutenant Commander John Lawrence, as they fight the Japanese.

programme.title: Bridge of Spies: Interview Special
programme.desc: Director Steven Spielberg and stars Tom Hanks and Mark Rylance talk about making their Cold War espionage drama.

programme.title: Spies in Disguise
programme.desc: A charming super spy and his geeky scientist companion are almost exact opposites. But what the scientist lacks in social skills he makes up for in invention, creating awesome gadgets for the spy to use on his epic missions.

programme.title: The Lavender Hill Mob
programme.desc: Henry Holland is a fussy supervisor who oversees gold bullion deliveries to the bank in which he works. Secretly, he is plotting to steal a load of bullion and retire early, but he cannot figure out a way to smuggle it out of the country.

programme.title: Sister Act
programme.desc: A cabaret singer witnesses a murder and goes undercover as a nun. In the convent, she is put in charge of coaching the choir, which she transforms into a toe-tapping rhythm group. As a result, the church's congregation swells until even the Pope comes to witness pop classics such as `My Guy' performed to glorify the Lord, but of course, her fame gives away her hiding place to the killer.

programme.title: Sister Act 2: Back in the Habit
programme.desc: Sequel in which nightclub singer Deloris Van Cartier is asked to get back into her habit when her old friends at St Catherine's Convent find difficulty in controlling a school filled with juvenile delinquents. Convincing everyone that Deloris is a nun is going to be a challenge in itself, especially with a group of incompetent monks trying to hinder their efforts.

programme.title: Braveheart
programme.desc: William Wallace is the medieval Scottish patriot who is spurred into revolt against the English when the love of his life is slaughtered. Leading his army into battles that become a war, his advance into England threatens King Edward I's throne before he is captured and executed, but not before becoming a symbol for a free Scotland.

programme.title: Benedetta
programme.desc: A 17th-century nun becomes entangled in a forbidden lesbian affair, but it's her shocking religious visions that threaten to shake the Church to its very core.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.sub-title: From Bunny Chow to Chicken Oysters
programme.desc: Guy Fieri's digging into the unexpected! A South African spot in Black Mountain, NC, is cooking lights-out lamb curry, a chef in San Jose, CA, is bringing big flavour to beef barbacoa, and a Boynton Beach, FL, joint is serving up chicken oysters.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. In Kentucky Guy visits a 1951 burger drive-in, a diner in Florida for meatloaf and a 60-year-old breakfast spot in Minnesota.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a pub in Philadelphia for fried empanadas, a spot in Washington for stuffed pork chops, and a seafood joint in Maine.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree is having a night on the town with Ladd, so she's prepping dinner for the kids in advance. She makes a slam-in-the-oven mini meatballs pasta bake and a kit for make-your-own-salad, with mixed berry shortcake for dessert.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: On the menu are divine ham and leak mini quiches, garden spread finger sandwiches, lemon raspberry tarts, mini doughnut muffins and fancy hot chocolate.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's boxing night, so Ree's running her sons to town for practice. She bakes them fruity granola chocolate chip cookies and prepares a knockout dinner of Italian sloppy joes, garlic toast and a fabulous lemon meringue pie.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree works on great new ideas for cooking low carb! She prepares grilled pork tenderloin with baby bok choy, cauliflower pizza crust and spicy pizza sauce, roasted shrimp with cherry tomatoes and roasted lemon-chili broccoli.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's dinner for two with Ree and her husband, Ladd, who's turning into a cowboy cook in the kitchen! They whip up fried shrimp with cocktail sauce, spicy curly fries, grilled corn casserole and Mexican chocolate sundaes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's mash-up mania on the ranch, and Ree proves two is better than one! She merges Italian and Mexican in chicken enchilasagne, then creates courgette bread waffles, potato chip and chocolate chip cookies, and a cheeseburger salad.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue is joined in her kitchen by award-winning musician YolanDa Brown for dishes that include a classic French stew and a family favourite of YolanDa's.

programme.title: Jamie's Great Britain
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver travels Britain to find out what makes our food great. He's searching for new ideas and inspiration to create his own versions of British classics. He kicks off his road-trip close to home in the East End, uncovering his family's roots in the area and tasting classic pie and mash and modern Vietnamese street food, as well as meeting a chef turning humble burgers into gourmet cuisine.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James and his wingman, top chef Mark Jordan, hop aboard their motorbikes to discover the wonderful island of Guernsey. They sample local speciality Gache cake, a seafood platter fit for a king, and James uses Guernsey's wonderful dairy produce to make a delicious creamy rice pudding with chargrilled peaches. Finally, James and Mark set themselves a fishing challenge by attempting to catch the King of the Sea: the mighty turbot.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James discovers the delights of Dorset. He is joined on his trip by his mate and Michelin-starred chef Paul Ainsworth. They hop aboard a millionaire's yacht, where James cooks a delicious sea bass dish. Paul discovers some fantastic local cheese, while James visits a breeder with mangalista pigs in a beautiful coastline setting. The highlight of their day is a beachside barbecue, where they feast on some amazing pork and chicken cooked al fresco.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick travels to Bamburgh and tastes some Scotch Pies. After visiting Mike Ainsley, who farms Cheviot sheep, Rick whips up a tasty moussaka, using local lamb.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and his dog Chalky are in the Yorkshire Dales. At Leyburn market, they check out the local dairy produce and, after chatting to the stall-holders, Rick prepares a Yorkshire curd tart.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits John Firth, who rears freshwater crayfish in Galloway, and cooks crayfish with pasta. He then travels to the picturesque town of Ludlow in Shropshire, which is as rich in gastronomic delights as it is in history.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits Cartmel in the Lake District, famous for its ancient 13th century Priory and Jean Johns' sticky toffee pudding. From her village shop in this tiny pretty village, truckloads of puddings are transported daily to all parts of Britain.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick visits Cork in Southern Ireland. At the English Market, the gastronomic heart of the city, he talks to retailers and cooks corned beef and cabbage. Still in Eire, he travels up the east coast to Waterford, a town usually renowned for its glass.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick is on home turf when he visits the Tintagel area in Cornwall. Here, legend has it, the ruined castle atop the craggy, untamed cliffs is what remains of King Arthur's Camelot.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: In the Clovelly area of North Devon, Rick goes fishing for herring with Steve Perham, whose family have been fishermen for over 200 years. All that fresh sea air gives them a hearty appetite as Rick fries the herring in wholemeal flour.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and Chalky travel to Loch Torridon in Scotland to go fishing with John McGreggor for langoustines. Here the prawns are protected by the Marien Stewardship Council, which has awarded its seal of approval to the fishermen.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick pays a memorable visit to the Channel Island of Jersey, where he goes reef fishing for sea bass, which he then grills. He also makes a consommé and enjoys a beach barbecue.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick takes a moonlit excursion off Newlyn Bay in Cornwall to go pilchard fishing with Nick Howell. Back on the beach, Rick barbecues the sardines and makes a piri piri sauce to accompany them.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Prue is joined in her kitchen by award-winning musician YolanDa Brown for dishes that include a classic French stew and a family favourite of YolanDa's.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri digs in and pigs out on all things pork. After sampling a unique take on tacos in San Antonio, he heads to New Orleans to try pork knuckles.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is exploring an entire world of barbecue. After sampling smokin' chicken salad in Delaware he heads to Kauai for a traditional Hawaiian pig roast. In San Diego, a real-deal joint with Argentinean flavour.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: An Italian joint sells arancini and veal scaloppini. A street food spot serves shawarma and falafel.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: A true British pub serves authentic English staples. A poke counter dishes out a Hawaiian classic.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy finds a Po-Boy shop in New Orleans, a restaurant serving fresh pasta in Spokane, Washington, and a barbecue spot in Minnesota.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy finds a fish shack in New Jersey, that flies in fish from the Amazon, a small wood-fired pizza spot in Long Island.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mexican cafe in New Jersey for chicken wings, a pizza spot in Idaho, and a Jamaican place in Nashville.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Kentucky Guy visits a sandwich joint with home made bread, tries Cajun coastal flavours in Georgia, and pizza in Washington, DC.

programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a classic restaurant in Nashville, a smoked barbecue joint in Georgia, and a seafood chowder and lobster spot in Maine.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 12 of San Jose's hottest hot wings without taking a drink - for five minutes after the food is gone.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 17 overstuffed hot dogs in less than half an hour.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Florida, Adam visits an English-style seaside eatery and an Amish restaurant.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Durham, North Carolina, Adam competes in the Doughman, a charity relay race that gives a whole new meaning to `eat and run'.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In the Lone Star State, Adam enjoys some mouth-watering, Texas-sized grub. Can he put away a 48 ounce steak?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Denver, Adam tackles a giant breakfast burrito containing seven potatoes, a pound of ham and 12 eggs.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Arriving in Minneapolis, Adam attempts to eat a metre of bratwurst and sauerkraut.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, can Adam manage to put away a six pound, two foot long Burrito?

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Charleston, South Carolina, Adam gets a big serving of Southern charm and an even bigger helping of low country cuisine.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Alaska Adam's belt-busting challenges include a seven-course meal that must be devoured in ninety minutes.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by top TV chef Monica Galetti. They're in Oxford, where Monica discovers the delights of an organic local distiller. James takes a river trip to a fantastic pick your own farm where he picks some raspberries and he uses them to cook a delicious pudding on the banks of the river. James also visits one of the world's best chefs, Raymond Blanc, at his restaurant Le Manoir aux Quat'Saisons, where he enjoys some of Raymond's Michelin-starred dishes.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James is in South Wales, accompanied by Michelin-starred chef Stephen Terry. James discovers award-winning Welsh wine and cooks a Welsh lamb ragu on a beautiful mountainside. He samples the delights of organic Welsh chocolate and visits cooking legend Shaun Hill at his renowned restaurant The Walnut Tree. The day comes to an end at Barry Island, where James and Stephen enjoy all the fairground rides at this famous resort and James cooks Barry Island churros overlooking the beach.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's the semi-final, and the remaining bakers are tested on their patisserie skills, with a domed Signature dish, a towering Technical and a display case Showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The stakes couldn't be higher as Paul and Prue set three challenges that test every aspect of the bakers' skills. Who will be crowned 2019's Bake Off champion?

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bake Off's back with a twist, as 12 new bakers form a bubble and enter the tent. It's Cake Week and the bakers tackle Battenberg, a turning-out technical and a heroic showstopper.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Biscuit Week and the bakers make a sophisticated fruit, nut and chocolate signature, produce two tropical technicals, and create an elaborate table setting showstopper.

programme.title: James Martin's Spanish Adventure
programme.desc: In this episode, James visits a huge indoor food market in Bilbao, takes a funicular to a famous steak restaurant, and heads to a winery on a seabed - we also see how La Carolina is made as James cooks cod, squid ink arancini, Basque cheesecake and seafood on a boat.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Adam must eat 17 overstuffed hot dogs in less than half an hour.

programme.title: Man v Food

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is bringing his culinary tour of the UK to his neck of the woods as he spends precious time in his adopted home county of Hampshire. He's taking his gun dog Cooper to a training session at Laverstoke before cooking an amazing seasonal dish of partridge with vegetables and a sloe gin sauce. James hangs out with his best friend Jody Scheckter, an ex-formula one racer with an epic car collection, and a surprising passion for buffalo mozzarella.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight
programme.desc: Patrick chairs two hours of spirited debate and news making interviews. Top guests tackle the issues that matter, with a sharp take on every story.

programme.title: Patrick Christys Tonight Late Edition
programme.desc: Patrick Christys explore tomorrow's news, tonight, including all the major stories making the front pages and full coverage of all the breaking news at home and abroad.

programme.title: Breakfast with Stephen and Anne
programme.desc: The Great British Breakfast continues into the weekend with Stephen Dixon and Anne Diamond. Prepare yourself for the weekend with a full round-up of the week's news and politics and sport.

programme.title: Trooping the Colour
programme.desc: Join Andrew Pearce and Ellie Costello, with Cameron Walker at Buckingham Palace for full coverage of the parade to mark the King's official birthday.

programme.title: The Weekend
programme.desc: A lively look at all the latest live and breaking news for the weekend from around the UK with our network of GB News reporters, plus views and debate from a wide range of perspectives.

programme.title: Nana Akua
programme.desc: Nana Akua presents her own unique take on the biggest stories of the day.

programme.title: The Saturday Five
programme.desc: Join our provocative panel to discuss and debate the spikiest stories and craziest controversies of the week.

programme.title: The Saturday Five Extra
programme.desc: Join our provocative panel to discuss and debate the spikiest stories and controversies of the week.

programme.title: Alex Armstrong Tonight
programme.desc: Alex Armstrong offers a two-hour deep dive into the latest UK politics and current affairs.

programme.title: Alex Armstrong Tonight Late Edition
programme.desc: Alex Armstrong presents UK politics, news, and current affairs, covering all the front-page stories.

programme.title: Alex Armstrong Tonight
programme.desc: Alex Armstrong offers a two-hour deep dive into the latest UK politics and current affairs.

programme.title: Alex Armstrong Tonight
programme.desc: Alex Armstrong offers a two-hour deep dive into the latest UK politics and current affairs.

programme.title: Door of Faith with James Corbett
programme.desc: Door of Faith Ministries and Pastor James Corbett base their works and teachings from Acts 14:27 - Highlighting the importance of Community and sharing the Work and Word of God.

programme.title: Amazing Facts
programme.desc: Pastor Doug Batchelor explores the Bible by getting practical guidance into Christian living and prophecy.

programme.title: Through the Bible - Andrew Wommack
programme.desc: Andrew Wommack presents this new show moving through scripture step by step, based on his Bible Commentaries garnered from his years of Ministry.

programme.title: Desire of All Nations
programme.desc: Nathan Morris of Shake The Nations is a British evangelist who has been on GOD TV since the Bay of the Holy Spirit Revival. He is known for his passionate messages on the Blood of Jesus and the Cross.

programme.title: Jerusalem Dateline
programme.desc: Explores archaeological work and discoveries in Israel. Includes a look at a Western Wall tunnel, restoration work on an ancient site, and treasures from the early days of Christianity.

programme.title: The Cry with Betty King
programme.desc: Reverend Betty King hosts a weekly series with a cry to the Lord through prayer, worship and discussions; born from Isaiah 58:1.

programme.title: Revive Nations With Shyju Mathew
programme.desc: A diverse schedule of international Christian programming with a bold, modern focus.

programme.title: Victory Today - Sam Luke
programme.desc: Victory Tabernacle is dedicated to proclaiming the gospel of Jesus Christ, empowered by the Spirit of Pentecost, with vision rooted in comprehension of God's divine purpose for His church. Pastor Sam Luke offers a religious discourse.

programme.title: Amazing Facts
programme.desc: Pastor Doug Batchelor explores the Bible by getting practical guidance into Christian living and prophecy.

programme.title: Glorious Moments -- Prophet Jerome
programme.desc: Bishop HE Rt Rev Prophet Dr Jerome Fernando answered the call of God over his life at a young age, and since then has had a heart to draw people to their rightful destinies in the Lord.

programme.title: CBN Superbook
programme.desc: Chris, Joy and Gizmo go inside a magical Bible and experience the events written in it.

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari is scared of playing her trumpet in front of the Treasure Champs, but Barry reminds her how he had courage to buy her trumpet in the first place. Barry also shows courage by going into a dark cave where a funny creature lives.

programme.title: The Mum Show
programme.desc: An honest, grace filled, soul searching, light hearted look at being a mum in today's world; Join Marina, Emma and Clare as they explore a biblical model of grace filled parenting and consider the unique mum-identity God has over every mums life.

programme.title: Courts Of Heaven
programme.desc: Troy Brewer speaks about timelines and how they can bring redemption to any timeframe that believers are living in and the need to learn different ways God can speak through the timing of events.

programme.title: The Cry with Betty King
programme.desc: Reverend Betty King hosts a weekly series with a cry to the Lord through prayer, worship and discussions; born from Isaiah 58:1.

programme.title: Order My Steps
programme.desc: Bishop John Anthony Francis challenges the Body of Christ to shake off complacency.

programme.title: ORU Chapel
programme.desc: The late evangelist Oral Roberts was the first to televise God's healing power at work in 1955. Today his university, ORU takes up his call to Go where the light is dim, and my voice is heard small.

programme.title: Katherine Ruonala
programme.desc: Hosted by Katherine Ruonala.

programme.title: The Lord's Challenge

programme.title: Tree of Life Church
programme.desc: Tree of Life Church is committed to making disciples that make disciples and are founded on the Complete Work of Christ.

programme.title: Jonathan Bernis: Jewish Voice
programme.desc: Explore the Jewish Roots of your Christian Faith, and learn how Bible prophecy and today's world events surrounding Israel are connected to your life.

programme.title: Parent Compass
programme.desc: Mothers and fathers with differing issues, races, and income status talk about their own upbringings and pasts, marriage, child rearing, faith and life management.

programme.title: Parables: Stories that Jesus Told

programme.title: Road Trip to Truth
programme.desc: John Fabarez talks with experts to help today's youth understand exactly who God is, what his word says and why they should trust it.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's desserts day in the finals, and the last chance for the competing chefs to impress the judges and veteran Angela Hartnett. After a hard-fought week, the end of this programme will see the judges finally reveal the winning four dishes which will make up this year's `Great British Menu'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This final programme follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the Olympic banquet at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, as they make the painstaking preparations for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook. The four winning chefs prepare an outstanding four-course menu for a truly Olympian guest list.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The spotlight is on Scotland. Returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway are all determined to get one of their dishes to the banquet. Will a witty take on spiced ham score highly, or will a warm chicken salad with a twist or a rabbit terrine come out on top? Veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE will be judging.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway cook their fish dishes. Will a traditional Scottish soup, a chowder or a risky no-cook mackerel dish impress veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The competition heats up as the three returning Scottish contenders, Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway, cook their main courses. An African inspired goat tagine, a beef dish inspired by a childhood cartoon and a duck dish with a comedy twist are dished up for veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway have just one more chance to impress veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE and win their place in front of the judges. Will a comedy custard pie, a sophisticated chocolate dessert or doughnuts inspired by a Scottish tradition come out on top?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Only one chef can make it through to the national finals to represent Scotland and cook at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in the North West. How will Aiden Byrne's dish entitled `Mad as a Box of Frogs' go down with veteran Phil Howard? Will he trounce Mary-Ellen McTague's crab starter or will Chris Holland's `Oriental style beef broth with a twis' score more highly with Phil?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North West chefs cook their fish courses. Will Aiden Byrne's `The Culinary Joke' - a take on a classic prawn cocktail - raise a laugh, or will Mary-Ellen McTague's dish of dover sole or Chris Holland's `Gone Fishing' salmon dish prove winners?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course. Will Aiden Byrne's witty beef dish entitled `Long Horn Beef with Noodle Pot' prove a winner, or will Mary-Ellen McTague's goat dish called `Our Kid' raise a smile? Perhaps Chris Holland will score highly with his dish called `A Tasting of Pork'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chef's last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu' judging panel. Which dessert will help get them through? Will it be Aiden Byrne's pineapple dish called `The Sweet Burger', Mary-Ellen McTague's take on a trifle called `Chamomile Lawn' or Chris Holland's dish called `Going Bananas'?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the North West. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. The judges are joined by celebrity guest judge writer, presenter and performer Rowland Rivron.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In central England, three culinary heavyweights return to the `Great British Menu' kitchen. Two Michelin star chef Daniel Clifford creates a dish called `A Trip to the Chippy', where nothing is as it first appears. That is also the theme for Michelin star chef Richard Bainbridge's starter, `banana split', while Will Holland offers up goats cheese mousse, balsamic jelly and gingerbread.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Here, the chefs try to impress with their fish courses. Daniel Clifford puts up a creative dish called `Catch of the Day'. Does Richard Bainbridge's dish titled `Tongue in Cheek' live up to its comedy name? Will judge Marcus Wareing find the comedy value in Will Holland's coconut poached brill, crab, celeriac and tomato essence?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England cook their main courses. Daniel Clifford's main has the same title as Richard Bainbridge's fish course, `Tongue in Cheek', but it is a wildly different plate of food; Richard Bainbridge presents guinea fowl in a beef bladder and calls the dish `Which Came First: the Chicken or the Egg'; Will Holland offers roast longhorn beef rib eye, bread and bone marrow pudding and parsley paste.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England have one last chance to impress Marcus Wareing before the final two are chosen to face the judging panel. Will Daniel Clifford's `Going Out with a Bang' be good enough? Has Richard Bainbridge dropped the comedy ball with his coffee and chocolate dessert called `Time to Reflect'? Or will an unusual dish of parsnip banoffee cake, chocolate tart and parsnip ice cream get Will Holland through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the central England region. Only two chefs remain to cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The panel is joined by guest judge, comedian and actor Jim Moir, known to millions as Vic Reeves.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs battle it out for a chance to cook at the special banquet. Will veteran judge Tom Kerridge like Chris Eden's modern twist on `Duck a l'Orange', or will he favour Peter Sanchez-Iglesias' dish called `Picnic'? Perhaps Emily Watkins's witty dish called `Eggs and Ham' will prove a winner.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their fish courses here. Cornishman Chris Eden takes inspiration from home to create a lobster and oyster dish called `Gone Surfing', Peter Sanchez-Iglesias cooks up a dish inspired by childhood memories titled `Fish and Chips', while Emily Watkins serves up a dish with cured trout called `High Tea'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The South West chefs cook their main courses and veteran Tom Kerridge is looking for perfection. Will Chris Eden's spin on a fast food classic simply called `B.L.T' gain top points? Or will Peter Sanchez Iglesias' nostalgic dish `Roast Dinner' or Emily Watkins' venison dish `Shoot Lunch' prove a winner?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For the South West chefs, which dessert will progress? Peach melba, apple pie and `Lolly and Sweets' fight it out.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the South West and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton and their guest, writer and performer Simon Day? The judges aren't easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Davies's chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrist's peas and ham or Luke Thomas's ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape'. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs prepare their main courses, but will any of them impress veteran Jeremy Lee enough to give them a top score? Richard Davies's dish is simply called `A Tasting of Pork', Mary Ann Gilchrist's `Three's a Crowd' doesn't give much away, while Luke Thomas's dish is called `Beef, Bone Marrow and Watercress'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the final chance for the Welsh chefs to impress Jeremy Lee with their dessert dishes. Which creation will help get two chefs through to the judging show? Will it be Richard Davies's strawberries and cream with a Comic Relief twist, or will Mary Ann Gilchrist's `Some Like It Hot' or Luke Thomas's rhubarb and custard get them through?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Great British Menu continues as 24 of the nation's finest chefs battle it out to cook gastronomic masterpieces that are also witty and fun. Their dishes have to put a smile on faces as they compete for the prize of cooking a dish at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall celebrating 25 years of Comic Relief's Red Nose Day appeals. It is decision time for the chefs representing Wales.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three North East chefs all want to top the leader board on day one with their starter. How will Colin McGurran's tomato gazpacho and garden salad perform? Stephanie Moon thinks she also has a chance with a witty chicken dish called `Why Did the Chicken...', and Charlie Lakin offers a controversial duck dish called `Mind Your Step'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their fish courses. Will former champion Colin McGurran's dish of cockles and mussels with salmon, inspired by his childhood memories, also inspire veteran judge Jason Atherton? Or perhaps Stephanie Moon's fish dish with a nod to one of the most famous gags of all time, crab custard pie, will be the winner? Charlie Lakin is also confident with his creation called `Tongue in Cheek'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs cook their main course and veteran Jason Atherton is looking for perfection. Will Colin McGurran's dish of duck and turnips be funnier than it sounds? Has Stephanie Moon dropped the comedy ball with her venison dish called `Behind the Fun is Some Serious Game'? Will Charlie Lakin's kebab inspired dish `A Night on the Tiles' raise a smile?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the North East chefs' last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu' judging panel. Which desserts will help get two chefs through to the showdown? Will it be Colin McGurran's tiramisu, Stephanie Moon's tricky pulled sugar dome, or Charlie Larkin's custard pie?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from the North East. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. They must now cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and their guest, comedian Tim Brooke-Taylor. The judges aren't easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in Northern Ireland. How will Chris Fearon cope competing against his former head chef Raymond McArdle? Will rising star Ian Orr be caught up in the middle? Both Chris and Raymond create very different takes on soup while Ian comes up with a rabbit dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Northern Ireland chefs cook their fish courses. Chris Fearon serves up a creation called `Stitched up Like a Kipper', while Raymond McArdle draws inspiration from a view from home and serves turbot called `A Day at the Beach'. Ian Orr creates a dish with a play on an old joke called `Why Did the Scallop Cross the Roe'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's time for the main course. Chris Fearon's dish called `Cowboy Supper' is inspired by the Wild West. Raymond McArdle creates a dish called `Titanic Smoked Venison, Tongue and Cheek', and Ian Orr cooks lamb and baked hay.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the chefs' last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu' judging panel. Which dessert will it be? Chris Fearon's dish inspired by comedy legend Tommy Cooper called `Just Like That?' competes against Raymond McArdle's `Chocolate Volcano Mountain' and Ian Orr's `Passion Egg'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's decision time for the chefs from Northern Ireland. One chef has left the competition and only two remain. Who will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton? They will be joined by celebrity guest judge writer and performer Charlie Higson and the judges aren't easily pleased.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs raise the curtain with their starters, but which one is going to produce a showstopper? All three contestants have created sophisticated plates of food with simple dish titles. Will veteran Jeremy Lee be impressed with Richard Davies's chicken salad or will Mary Ann Gilchrist's peas and ham or Luke Thomas's ham, egg and pineapple get them off to a flying start?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Welsh chefs cook their fish course. Richard Davies serves up grilled and tartare of mackerel, while Mary Ann Gilchrist serves a lobster dish and calls it `The Great Escape'. Luke Thomas is hoping that his quirky take on a classic fish finger will impress veteran Jeremy Lee too.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the main course and rabbit, rose veal and venison are all dished up for `Great British Menu' veteran Richard Corrigan. Plus, one of the chefs is joined by `Eastenders' star June Brown for a fundraising challenge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course. Will a strawberry and coconut dessert, a frozen white chocolate dish or apple and cherries come out on top? `Great British Menu' veteran judge Richard Corrigan will decide who goes through to the judges tomorrow.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from London and the South East. One chef left the competition last time and only two remain. They will cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu' judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. This time they will be joined by celebrity guest judge, comedienne and writer Arabella Weir.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is finals week and 24 of the nation's finest chefs have been whittled down to just eight. Over the course of four hour-long shows, they will battle it out in the kitchen for the chance to get a dish to the Comic Relief Banquet. Actress Patricia Hodge, one of the stars of sitcom `Miranda', joins judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort to taste all eight winning regional starter courses.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers things at Norwich Castle Museum which highlight facts about Tudor life, Anglo-Saxon childbirth rituals, and the Pompeii of the Bronze Age.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: There's a family home in Consett in County Durham, a rental investment in Derby and an auction buy in Beckenham in London which will be used to run a new business.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion looks round a large Victorian terrace in Plumstead, Martin is in Mickleover in Derby hearing a father and daughter's plans for a flat and Lucy discovers what happened to a plot of land she saw five and a half years ago.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a bungalow in a stunning setting in the Lake District while Dion has a look at a flat close to Nottingham city centre. Lucy meets a woman who mistakenly thought she was buying a piece of land in Folkestone in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks at a flat in Kilburn in London with one of the worst cases of damp she has ever seen, while Martin and Dion are in the Midlands looking round semis with potential.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat with huge potential in London's Earlsfield, and terraced houses in Wombourne near Wolverhampton and Ynyswen near Treorchy in Wales are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is impressed with the potential of two shops and three flats in Tonypandy in Wales while Dion looks round a two-bedroom maisonette in Dawley and Lucy meets a first time developer with ambitious plans for an end terrace in London's Forest Gate.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A first-floor one-bedroom flat in London's Stepney Green, a three-bed semi in Dartford in Kent and a one-bedroom flat on the 17th floor of a block in Salford are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s bungalow in Dereham in Norfolk, a first-floor flat in London's East Ham and a property in Maryport in Cumbria with a hidden room are all sold at auction. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear their plans for their investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a semi in Ilkeston in Derby, a flat in Streatham Hill in London and a bungalow in Somerset's Portishead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin, Lucy and Dion visit a fifth-floor flat in Greater Manchester, a three bed semi in Carlisle and three commercial buildings near Stanstead.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An old Derbyshire farmhouse in Wirksworth gets a stunning make over, a developer builds a pair of semis in Chatham in Kent while a mid-terraced house in Blackhall Colliery in County Durham is given a makeover ready for the rental market.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in London's Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in London's Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if it'll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers' cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house with a bathroom layout challenge in Ripley, Derbyshire, a huge three-storey house with its freehold on offer in Erdington, Birmingham, and a mid-terrace house in London's East Ham in need of a major makeover are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: American Monster

programme.title: Dermot's Taste of Ireland
programme.desc: Dermot travels to Letterkenny in Donegal, often called Ireland's 'forgotten county', but quickly discovers a place full of unforgettable people, stories and food. Surrounded by ancient rainforest, he heads into the woods to forage for prized golden chanterelle mushrooms before visiting the renowned Lemon Tree Restaurant, where he learns to make tortellini completely from scratch with one of Donegal's most skilled chefs.

programme.title: Dr. No

programme.desc: Ainsley rustles up recipes packed with vibrant flavours and citrusy kicks that'll put a real spring in your step! JB Gill also joins him for a lesson in making the perfect fruit pavlova.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning

programme.title: Dermot's Taste of Ireland
programme.desc: Dermot heads to the wild west of Donegal to explore the county's Irish-speaking Gaeltacht, where the people, music and food are anything but ordinary. After a crash course at Glencolmcille Folk Village - a living museum celebrating old Irish traditions - Dermot bravely puts his Irish to the test ordering a pint in the local pub. He also discovers an old Irish cure-all: carrageen moss pudding, a surprisingly delicious seaweed dessert said to keep everything from colds to heartbreak at bay.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands to Highlands
programme.desc: James takes a magical trip to the stunning Isle of Skye, where he's joined by top chef and ultimate Scotsman Tony Singh. Tony visits a local bakery which doubles as an art gallery and learns to make the local oatcakes. James is inspired by the amazing local seafood and cooks up a tasty dish using plaice and lovage. Their day comes to a perfect end aboard a boat, where they spot dozens of dolphins, and James cooks up a fresh lobster and langoustine dish with an added Scottish twist.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: James visits Languedoc, famous for its wine and the hearty meaty dish, cassoulet. He turns up the heat with a steak cooked on a barbecue in the blazing sunshine.

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure

programme.title: Ainsley's Fantastic Flavours
programme.desc: With bright colours, big tastes and dishes that add a little sunshine to every bite, Ainsley brings sunny vibes straight to your table. From Greek chicken gyros to mozzarella panzanella with a fruity twist, these recipes are perfect for those long, lazy days and holiday get-togethers! Lisa Snowdon pops in to make her summertime favourite seafood linguine, and chef Joseph Denison Carey goes strawberry picking in the sun.

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen

Elsewhere, John heads off to Larkhill Vineyard to pick up some tips on becoming a successful wine producer while Prue has a mouthwatering hack for garlic confit which can be stored in a jar.

programme.title: The 1% Club
programme.desc: Lee Mack presents the game show where the questions aren't about general knowledge but logic, brainpower and common sense. 100 contestants begin each edition, who'll face the final question, which only 1 per cent of the population can get right.

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: It was an electric week in the villa, and this is your chance to catch up on all the moments they didn't have time to show. All the flirting, all the fallouts and all the drama is right here.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After Stewie is banished from his grandparents' house, Brian takes a trip to retrieve the terrible tyke.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: After writing a story for a local magazine, Brian lands a job with the prestigious `New Yorker'. However, his college dropout status haunts him so he decides to go back to Brown University to get his degree. As Brian struggles to pass his class, Stewie enjoys the college lifestyle. Meanwhile, Peter and his friends combine forces to help the city.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan goes to Atlantic City for a bachelor party and is shunned by his so-called best friend. Roger steps in and becomes Stan's new sidekick. Their friendship goes to a whole new level, but when they return home and face Francine, Stan realises that what happens in Atlantic City doesn't necessarily stay there.

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: When Stan's father passes away, Stan reveals his real father is actually Jack Smith, a super-suave spy.

programme.title: G'wed
programme.desc: Reece wakes from a surreal nightmare to find Disneyland Darren standing in his bedroom after Jodie lets him stay in the spare room. His return sparks tension with Mr Dunn, now dating Jodie, and Darren's chaos disrupts the household. At school, it's Refugee Awareness Week led by the woke Brimble. After derailing events with pranks, Reece is forced by headmaster Meachy to help her, but another prank goes too far and publicly humiliates her, leaving him feeling unexpectedly guilty.

programme.title: Plebs
programme.desc: The boys begin an archaeological dig after Grumio unearths an brooch whilst on the loo. They try to keep their activity a secret but others cotton on. When a professor of archaeology goes missing, the boys, who paid him a recent visit, are suspected.

programme.title: Piglets
programme.desc: Brand new comedy following six raw recruits as they start life at a police training college, with the key staff who have to knock them into shape. Stars Sarah Parish and Mark Heap.

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: On a visit to his stepmother, Morse finds himself in the middle of a tragedy - his step-niece has committed suicide. Morse takes compassionate leave, but begins searching into his step-niece's past to find reasons for her death.

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Morse investigates when the founder of a private hospital is found dead in his car. All fingers point to an ex-bookmaker whose daughter suffered brain damage during what should have been a routine hospital operation, and Morse finds himself touched by the man's devotion to his daughter - but must ensure his judgement is not clouded.

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Endeavour uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar accidents, which leads him to an all-women Oxford college determined to eschew co-education. Eventually, the investigation reaches right to the heart of CID.

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Miss Marple is faced with her greatest challenge when she receives instructions from a recently deceased friend, Mr Rafiel, to investigate a possible crime. Miss Marple finds herself on a mystery coach tour with her nephew Raymond West and a number of intriguing passengers.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual Midsomer Scarecrow Festival takes a grisly turn as bodies begin appearing on poles displayed alongside their straw creations; the investigation reveals rivalries that run deep and shows people will do just about anything to win.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Mummers, an amateur operatic society, rehearses for its charity concert when the body of an unknown man is found in the theatre; the investigation quickly becomes a race to discover who is desperate to take centre stage.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Each year, the village of Angel's Rise hosts the Psychic Fayre. Founded by the Saint-Stephens family in memory of their late daughter, the event attracts mediums, psychics and followers of the occult. When a body is found surrounded by ritual symbols on the eve of the festival, Barnaby must step into the spiritual world to find the killer.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: In the quiet rural village of Ferne Basset, an elderly resident is found drowned after a violent attack. Meanwhile, a local amateur dramatic production of `Amadeus' reveals a hitherto unseen world of intrigue and passion.

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: DI Vera Stanhope rescues a young boy from a freezing pond after he escapes from a violent attack which left his mother dead. As Vera and her team investigate what happened to the child, they unravel a web of deadly secrets and discover a murderous sequence of events set in motion by power, money and a mother's love. Although she is usually awkward and uninterested around children, Vera finds a kindred spirit in the boy.

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a brutal murder disturbs the tranquillity of an ancient Oxford college, Morse discovers that he has unwittingly provided all the suspects with a watertight alibi.

programme.title: Sharpe

programme.title: Sharpe

With the lives of his beloved wife and teenage son on the line, the only way James can save his family - and the world - is to try to realign the Earth's axis with strategically placed nuclear weapons.

Successful in the mission, his force are now the target of the Seminoles, led by Chief Oscala, whose knowledge of the Everglades is superior to that of the retreating army force. Wyatt struggles to keep his men together and safe from natural perils as well as enemy attacks.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Alan and Sylvie are first to host in their seafront establishment Watersedge Guest House in Barton on Sea, Hampshire. The first guests to arrive are Blackpool boys Mike and Danny of Homecliffe Hotel, who locate dust and grime enough to grumble. Next through the door are Tim and Amelia, owners of Nearwater B&B, St Mawes. Austrian-born Eva of the Roundhouse Hotel in Weymouth, loves her spacious garden room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's Tim and Amelia's turn to host the rival B&B owners at their non-star-rated boutique accommodation, Nearwater, on the south Cornish coast. After a detailed inspection from Mike and Danny, they find fuel for their fire in some rather surprising places. Eva's feeling confident about a good night's sleep after testing the mattress, and Sylvie and Alan are still trying to get their head around what `boutique' really means.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the turn of Austrian-born Eva Buschmann to host in her cosmopolitan boutique B&B on the Weymouth seafront, the Roundhouse Hotel. First to arrive are Danny and Mike who are a little shocked when Eva offers them a double room with an exposed shower.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Showmen Mike and Danny host their rival B&B owners on day four at their four-star rated guest house - Homecliffe Hotel - in Blackpool. After the harsh criticism Tim and Amelia received from Mike and Danny, the couple arrive at Homecliffe determined to find problems with the cleanliness of the room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day and all the B&B hosts gather for one last time to find out how much they've been paid. Watersedge hosts Alan and Sylvie kick things off, but ahead of discovering how much they've been paid, they're keen to question Tim and Amelia who complained about their room having no en suite.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Football fans Catherine and Martin are fulfilling their dream of buying a holiday home in Lazio, Italy. Scarlette Douglas lines up five options for £275k. Will any hit the back of the net?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Martyn and Sharon find their dream holiday home on Portugal's beautiful Algarve. Can they find the perfect place that all their friends and family can enjoy for £160k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jean Johansson helps Trish find a holiday home on the coast in southern Cyprus, where she can holiday with her friends and family and enjoy the island's warm winters, for her £90k.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Clover and Mark want to start a new life in the stunning white villages of Axarquía in Andalucía. Danni Menzies lines up five superb private villas with pools and stunning views for £300k.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Cardiff, DJ Trishna hopes her cooking will be even better than her rapping as she hosts a hip-hop themed night. But when Yuno shakes his booty on the dance floor, Nick is unimpressed.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second day of competition in Cardiff and tonight's host Nick is planning to bring some refinement and fine dining to the week's proceedings.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On day three in Cardiff, retail manager Yuno serves some traditional dancing alongside his Nepalese food. But will his spicy dishes be too fiery for his guests' delicate British palates?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day four in Cardiff and Michelle is out to impress with a culinary trip around the world, but will a third serving of lamb this week leave her guests bleating with despair?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night of the competition in Cardiff and host Suzanne is pinning her hopes on an evening of sophistication. Can Cardiff's queen of the saucy quip walk away with the prize?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at The Art Bank in Kirkcudbrightshire, where Mandy and Suzanne hope a dash of artistic flare will win them the week.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Amy and her friend Anna sort-of host the week's second visit in Cockermouth, Cumbria. Mandy and Suzanne dig up some issues from their own feedback during a visit to a mining museum.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Meru hope their guests will love their traditional seaside guest house, The Sanderling in Morecambe. A walk along the Morecambe coast surprises some of the B&Bers, and a well-received bargain brekkie gives the hosts reason to be cheerful.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Hosts Lee and Janette think their combination of rooms and fine dining at The Curious Fox in Skipton, North Yorkshire, will wow their fellow competitors. However, an unwelcome discovery in one room could scupper them.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The new run continues. It's payment day. Toilet troubles, bathroom bothers and some very picky comments are all on the agenda before the payments are revealed and we find out who's won.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Iris, who's 91, comes to St George's with two severe leg fractures, and medics are worried that she may not survive. Elijah has injured a finger on a kebab slicer, and Alison has sepsis.

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics Jan Vann and Chelsie Kennedy rush to a road traffic collision. A suspected drink driver crashes into a lamp post, crews rush to a man with a dangerously high heart rate, and a two year old has a severe allergic reaction.

programme.title: 999: On the Frontline
programme.desc: Paramedics rush to a 10-year-old boy suffering a major epileptic fit. At the same time in Coventry, an elderly woman struggles to breathe and paramedics are called to a man with extreme anxiety.

programme.title: Teen Mom OG
programme.desc: For four seasons fans of `Teen Mom' watched Farrah, Maci, Amber and Catelynn face multiple challenges in their first years of motherhood, including such things as graduating from high school, relationships, getting jobs, beginning college and moving out on their own. Oh, and raising their first child. Now the original girls - hence, the `OG' in the revived series title - return to reveal the challenges of raising children on camera, and how life in the public eye has shaped them as parents and people. It's an unprecedented view of young mothers facing the responsibilities of parenthood as their children enter kindergarten, start little league and reach other milestones of youth.

programme.desc: Nazi dreams of an ideal city start to take shape. Neighbourhoods are slated for demolition, and the ideal Aryan city in Linz is designed with temples of art and culture looted from the downtrodden.

programme.title: Air Crash Investigation
programme.desc: Investigation into British Airways' flight number 38's crash near Heathrow in 2008. Two minutes before it touched down, double engine failure crippled the plane.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The introduction of the steam railways in the early 19th-century changed Britain in a way no one could have predicted. In this episode, the team explore how they created a domestic revolution, changing the way we lived, from the houses we lived in to the food we ate.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team visit Beamish in County Durham to explore how the railway companies soon recognised that there was money to be made in moving people, rather than just stone, coal and iron. The comfort of the early passenger wagons are put to the test on one of the earliest steam trains, and Ruth finds out how people were moving further than ever before.

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team find out how the railways transformed the British diet, rescuing a nation that was struggling to feed itself. Putting theory into practice Alex and Peter load a flock of sheep onto a train, discovering how the mass transportation of livestock by rail transformed the quality and quantity of meat available to Victorian consumers. This new capability gave birth to the traditional British roast.

programme.title: Duane Allman - Song of the South
programme.desc: Duane Allman, the lead guitarist and driving force behind Southern Rock pioneers The Allman Brothers Band, lived a tragically short life, but during his brief career he changed music forever.

programme.title: Lenny Kravitz - Live at the iTunes Festival
programme.desc: Lenny Kravitz on Sept. 26, 2014, at the iTunes Festival, giving his audience a great concert at the Roundhouse of London.

programme.title: James Taylor - Austin City Limits

programme.title: Green Day: Live at Fox Theater

programme.title: The Offspring: Rock AM

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, the pair visit two amazing sets of sheds in the Oxfordshire and Hampshire area. In the heart of Oxfordshire they find rare Minis, 1970s Rollers, and a pristine rally car. The Hampshire sheds are full to the rafters with Japanese bikes, but they come away with a real surprise. Selling their projects on, however, proves to be a real challenge.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, on the Cornish coast, Henry and Sam spend the day surrounded by vintage vehicles with genteel pedigrees including a the first ever Lotus Elan, and a pre-war Sunbeam. In Oxfordshire, Sam falls for a a Midget racer - but the restoration is a daunting task.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds a 2002 Chevy Corvette Z06 that can do zero to 60 in under four seconds. Ant is less than enthused when he realises the scale of the work needed.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finds an early 60's Cadillac with fins that is on sale at the equivalent of only £3000. Mike and Edd try to turn this ugly duckling into a swan.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Car dealer Mike Brewer snatches up a 1973 Ford Mustang Mach 1. Can mechanic Ant Anstead bring back the lost power by tuning up and enhancing the engine?

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.sub-title: This Horse Needs To Be Put Out of It's Misery
programme.desc: Mike decides to pay it forward and help out a young member of the team by fixing up his 4-liter V6 2007 Mustang. Ant and Mike soon find out that just because this young man works with cars doesn't mean he's taken all Mike's advice to heart.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: The Dodge SRT 10 truck has an 8.3 litre V10 Viper engine, and Mike finds one at a price he can't resist. Once Ant is sold on the plan, they dive in, working to make this vehicle powerful enough to dominate on a NASCAR track.

programme.desc: The baffling murder of a young woman leads police to two potential suspects: her new boyfriend and an obsessed stalker.

programme.desc: Documentary series examining what led Fred and Rose West to become serial killers.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders
programme.desc: Focuses on the notorious house which the couple shared in Gloucester. Fred and Rose murdered their first three victims - Anna McFall, Charmaine West and then Rena Costello - because they `got in the way'. But when they moved into 25 Cromwell Street, the couple began to kill for sexual thrills.

programme.title: Fred and Rose: The West Murders

programme.title: Fred & Rose West: Love & Murder
programme.desc: Fred and Rose West raped, tortured and murdered at least a dozen young British girls between 1967 and 1987, including several of their own daughters. programme.title: Myra Hindley: The Untold Story

programme.title: Myra Hindley: The Untold Story

programme.title: Heartland Homicide

programme.title: Y Wal

programme.title: Newyffion

programme.title: Twm Twrch

programme.title: Gwesty Aduniad

programme.title: The Myth of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Marilyn Monroe is the defining icon of the American 1950s. A seemingly fairytale transformation from rags to riches, a shining example of the mythical American dream. However, as the decade continued, the myth of Marilyn Monroe and the American Dream seemed to crumble.

programme.title: LSO: Rattle Conducts Brahms and Shostakovich
programme.desc: Sir Simon Rattle leads the London Symphony Orchestra in performing works by Brahms and Shostakovich.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An amiable swindler gets more than he bargained for when he buys a painting from a sweet old lady. Can she really be as naïve about the picture's worth as she seems?

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Two men are locked in a room together, and one is shot, but there is no sign of a gun.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: Gossip columnist Lionel Brewster lets fly with his witty one-liners and his victims want revenge. Luckily two young actors, down on their luck, are on hand to offer their clients much more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: A Girl Can't Always Have Everything
programme.desc: Two struggling theatre actresses have very different off-stage personas. Suzy is a gold-digger who soon begins milking the theatre's new backer for expensive gifts, but when he then wants to marry her, she calls on her more mild-mannered friend Pat for help. How loyal is she?

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: An amoral antique dealer masquerades as a man of the cloth to secure rock bottom prices in the name of a bogus charity, but his day of atonement may be nigh when he buys a rare item of furniture from an old farmer.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A curious woman tells the police her neighbour (Gary Merrill) has buried his wife's body in his back yard. With Fess Parker.

programme.title: Alfred Hitchcock Hour
programme.desc: A lawyer (Richard Basehart), once an actor, helps his son, who stands trial for homicide. With Florence Halop.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: An intimate profile of Karl Malden, the actor who won an Oscar for his role as Mitch in `A Streetcar Named Desire' and was nominated for another for `On the Waterfront'.

programme.title: Pavarotti in Central Park
programme.desc: Luciano Pavarotti performs in Central Park with flutist Andrea Griminelli, members of the New York Philharmonic and the Boys Choir of Harlem.

programme.title: Eric Clapton and Guests: Band Du Lac
programme.desc: Eric Clapton is joined by special guests, including Ringo Starr, Roger Taylor, Mike Rutherford and Katie Melua, to perform a set of greatest hits from Queen, Genesis and The Beatles.

programme.title: Jerry Lee Lewis and Friends

programme.title: Ray Charles - Live At Montreux
programme.desc: Live at the Montreux Jazz Festival first pressing or reissue. Complete your Ray Charles collection.

programme.title: Ella Fitzgerald: Just One Of Those Things
programme.desc: Exploring the life of jazz singer Ella Fitzgerald, showcasing her impact on civil rights and her lasting legacy.

programme.title: Amy Winehouse: I Told You I Was Trouble - Live At Shepherds Bush.
programme.desc: Fresh from Brit Awards and Ivor Novello wins, Amy Winehouse dazzles at Shepherd's Bush Empire, performing hits like Back to Black and Frank.

programme.title: Nina Simone: The Lost Album
programme.desc: Spotlighting a lost album from one of the most gifted vocalists of her generation, unearth new stories relating to the iconic singer-songwriter Nina Simone.

programme.title: Nina Simone: Live at Montreux 1976
programme.desc: Nina Simone performs live at Montreux, from 1976.

programme.title: Women Who Rock
programme.desc: The hard-won success of their predecessors enables stars like Shania Twain, Macy Gray and Sarah McLachlan to overcome obstacles and reach new heights; a glimpse of the new generation: Taylor Swift, St. Vincent, Syd, Billie Eilish, Yola and more.

programme.title: Morbius
programme.desc: Biochemist Michael Morbius tries to cure himself of a rare blood disease, but when his experiment goes wrong, he inadvertently infects himself with a form of vampirism instead.

programme.title: Safe House
programme.desc: Six government agents become trapped inside a high-security safe house after a terrorist attack in downtown Los Angeles. They soon realise they must survive not just the enemy outside, but the potential traitor among them.

programme.title: The Beekeeper
programme.desc: One man's brutal campaign for vengeance takes on national stakes after it's revealed he's a former operative of a powerful and clandestine organisation known as Beekeepers.

programme.title: Red Right Hand
programme.desc: Cash is trying to live a quiet, honest life in a small Appalachian town. When a vicious crime boss forces him back into her services, he soon learns he's capable of anything to protect his family and his home.

programme.title: The Italian Job
programme.desc: A group of petty criminals plan to rig a major traffic jam to divert attention from their bank heist.

programme.title: Demolition Man
programme.desc: Violence has been outlawed in the politically correct regime of 2032, and the only weapons in existence are consigned to a museum, but when an escaped psychopath heads straight for the museum, the authorities decide the only effective recourse they have is to thaw out a violent, cryogenically frozen cop.

programme.title: Days of Thunder
programme.desc: A hotshot rookie stock car driver sets his sights on the professional NASCAR circuit and the prestigious Daytona 500.

programme.title: Superman
programme.desc: Tech billionaire Lex Luthor seizes the opportunity to get the Man of Steel out of the way for good. Will intrepid reporter Lois Lane and Superman's four-legged companion, Krypto, be able to help him before it's too late?

programme.title: The Running Man
programme.desc: Desperate for money to save his sick daughter, Ben Richards joins the top-rated show on television, "The Running Man," a deadly competition where contestants must survive 30 days while being hunted by professional assassins.

programme.title: Twisters
programme.desc: Competing teams of storm chasers find themselves in a fight for their lives as multiple systems converge over central Oklahoma.

programme.title: Fuze
programme.desc: London undergoes a citywide evacuation when a World War II bomb is unearthed at a construction site. Amid the escalating chaos, a daring criminal operation is set in motion -- one that uses the evacuation as a cover for a meticulously planned heist.

Stylish historical epic retelling the fierce battle at Thermopylae in 481BC. The Spartan king, Leonidas, and his tiny army of 300 followers provided inspiration for the whole of Greece as they fought the massed ranks of the Persian army whose commander, King Xerxes, had up to a million warriors at his disposal.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger rides into a small frontier town in the American southwest, provoked into a gunfight in which he kills three men, and then begged by the townsfolk to become sheriff. The residents are terrified of the gunslinger but live in greater fear of a gang which intends to wipe out the whole town.

programme.title: The Warriors
programme.desc: Wrongly accused of assassinating top gang leader Cyrus at a rally, Coney Islanders The Warriors have to fight their way home over the course of one long, violent night.

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again
programme.desc: In 1979 Donna falls in love with Sam while embarking on a series of adventures throughout Europe. In the present day, Donna's pregnant daughter reunites with her mother's old friends and boyfriends on the Greek island of Kalokairi.

programme.title: The Tuxedo
programme.desc: An ordinary cabbie uses gadget-laden formal wear to combat a villain who wants to poison the world's water supply.

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective
programme.desc: Tale about an unconventional sleuth's attempts to find a football team's stolen mascot dolphin. The Miami Dolphins are aghast when their beloved Snowflake is abducted one week before the all-important Super Bowl, so they call in Ace Ventura, a private eye who uses unorthodox methods to retrieve missing animals.

programme.title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls
programme.desc: Sequel about the zany private detective who specialises in locating missing pets. Ace Ventura travels to Africa to search for a kidnapped sacred albino bat, which reputedly has the power to restore peace between two warring tribes.

programme.title: Yesterday
programme.desc: Jack Malik is a struggling singer-songwriter in an English seaside town whose dreams of fame are rapidly fading, despite the fierce devotion and support of his childhood best friend, Ellie. After a freak bus accident during a mysterious global blackout, Jack wakes up to discover that The Beatles have never existed. Performing songs by the greatest band in history to a world that has never heard them, Jack becomes on overnight sensation with a little help from his agent.

programme.title: Bridget Jones: The Edge of Reason
programme.desc: Bridget's brand new relationship with Darcy has taken a plummet due to her jealousy of his workmate. Having previously left the publishing house she worked for to avoid her boss, Daniel, with whom she had an unsuccessful romance, she finds him along for the ride on her new job, which takes her to Thailand, and almost into his arms.

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 3
programme.desc: After the highs of winning the world championships, the Bellas find themselves split apart. But when they get the chance to reunite for an overseas USO tour, this group of awesome nerds will come together to make some music one last time.

programme.title: Dumb and Dumberer: When Harry Met Lloyd
programme.desc: A grant-greedy principal puts two clueless teens in a bogus class for students with special needs.

programme.title: Eddie the Eagle
programme.desc: Guided by a rebellious and charismatic coach, British ski jumper Michael "Eddie" Edwards trains for the 1988 Winter Olympics in Calgary, Alberta.

programme.title: Instant Family
programme.desc: When Pete and Ellie decide to start a family, they stumble into the world of foster care adoption. They hope to take in one small child, but when they meet three siblings, including a rebellious 15-year-old girl, they find themselves speeding from zero to three kids overnight. Now, Pete and Ellie must try to learn the ropes of instant parenthood in the hope of becoming a family.

programme.title: Ace Ventura: Pet Detective
programme.desc: Tale about an unconventional sleuth's attempts to find a football team's stolen mascot dolphin. The Miami Dolphins are aghast when their beloved Snowflake is abducted one week before the all-important Super Bowl, so they call in Ace Ventura, a private eye who uses unorthodox methods to retrieve missing animals.

programme.title: Ace Ventura: When Nature Calls
programme.desc: Sequel about the zany private detective who specialises in locating missing pets. Ace Ventura travels to Africa to search for a kidnapped sacred albino bat, which reputedly has the power to restore peace between two warring tribes.

programme.title: Sausage Party
programme.desc: Supermarket perishables devise a plan to escape from their human enemies after Frank the sausage learns that they will eventually become meals.

programme.title: The 4:30 Movie
programme.desc: During the summer of 1986, three 16-year-old buddies spend their Saturdays sneaking into movies at the local cineplex. When one of the guys invites the girl of his dreams to see an R-rated film, all hilarity breaks loose as a self-important theatre manager and teenage rivalries interfere with his best-laid plans.
