programme.title: Touch
programme.desc: Based on the novel by Ólafur Jóhann Ólafsson.

programme.title: The End of the Affair
programme.desc: As war rages over London, neighbours Maurice Bendrix and Sarah Miles fall in love and begin a relationship. Unfortunately Sarah is married to Henry, a work colleague of the American Maurice, who confides in him that he thinks his wife is cheating on him.

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
programme.desc: A bittersweet story about a 43-year-old woman on the verge of divorce who magically travels back in time to her senior high-school years. She is able to act with the benefit of hindsight, but despite her efforts, she falls for the same boy all over again.

programme.title: The Room Next Door
programme.desc: Ingrid and Martha became close friends while working together at the same magazine. After years of being out of touch, they meet again in an extreme but strangely sweet situation.

programme.title: Girl You Know It's True
programme.desc: Grammy Award-winning music act Milli Vanilli become the first act to have their prize stripped from them after they are exposed as a fake enterprise with the singers lip-syncing to pre-recorded tracks.

programme.title: Erin Brockovich
programme.desc: Based on the true story of an unsophisticated single mother who stumbles upon an environmental cover-up. While working for a lawyer, she is puzzled by an old case and decides to do some research of her own. She discovers that a chemical company knowingly polluted the local area, and with the help of the lawyer launches a class action to sue the company for damages.

programme.title: Lost in Translation
programme.desc: In Tokyo shooting a whisky commercial, middle-aged actor Bob Harris becomes attracted to Charlotte, the young wife of a celebrity photographer. Together they ride out their boredom of hotel life and their bewilderment of the city.

programme.title: The Bodyguard
programme.desc: A Secret Service agent-turned-bodyguard finds danger and romance while protecting an entertainer from a stalker.

programme.title: Moneyball
programme.desc: Faced with a tight budget, a baseball manager and his partner recruit bargain bin players that scouts have labelled as flawed, but have game-winning potential.

programme.title: The Blind Side
programme.desc: Michael Oher is a poor and undereducated African-American teenager who lived all his life on the streets. His tremendous size and quickness makes him a perfect athlete and attracts the interest if a wealthy and conservative suburban family who take him in and groom him both athletically and academically.

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married

programme.title: The Room Next Door

programme.title: DC League of Super-Pets
programme.desc: Krypto the Super-Dog and Superman are inseparable best friends, now Krypto must convince a ragtag group of animals to master their own newfound powers to help him save Superman and the rest of the Justice League.

programme.title: Tafiti: Across the Desert
programme.desc: When his grandfather gets bitten by a snake, a young meerkat embarks on an adventure across the desert to find the magical flower that can cure him.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: Dog Man
programme.desc: A life-saving surgery fuses together a police officer and his faithful police dog -- and Dog Man is born. Learning to embrace his new identity, Dog Man must stop feline supervillain Petey the Cat from cloning himself and going on a crime spree.

programme.title: The Secret Life of Pets 2
programme.desc: Max the terrier encounters canine-intolerant cows, hostile foxes and a scary turkey when he visits the countryside. Luckily for Max, he soon catches a break when he meets Rooster, a gruff farm dog who tries to cure the lovable pooch of his neuroses.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Out of the Shadows
programme.desc: The turtles spring into action to battle Shredder, mad scientist Baxter Stockman, Bebop, Rocksteady and the notorious Krang.

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2
programme.desc: Reformed criminals Mr. Wolf, Mr. Snake, Mr. Piranha, Mr. Shark and Ms. Tarantula find themselves hijacked into a high-stakes heist that's masterminded by a new team of delinquents they never saw coming -- the Bad Girls.

programme.title: Paddington
programme.desc: A young Peruvian bear with a passion for all things British travels to London in search of a home. Finding himself lost and alone at Paddington Station, he begins to realise that city life is not all he had imagined - until he meets the kindly Brown family, who offer him a temporary haven.

programme.title: Paddington 2
programme.desc: Settled in with the Brown family, Paddington the bear is a popular member of the community who spreads joy and marmalade wherever he goes. One fine day, he spots a pop-up book in an antique shop: the perfect present for his beloved aunt's 100th birthday. When a thief steals the prized book, Paddington embarks on an epic quest to unmask the culprit before Aunt Lucy's big celebration.

programme.title: Ghostbusters: Afterlife
programme.desc: When a single mother and her two children move to a new town, they soon discover that they have a connection to the original Ghostbusters and their grandfather's secret legacy.

programme.title: Transformers One
programme.desc: Brothers-in-arms Orion Pax and D-16 become sworn enemies Optimus Prime and Megatron.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Out of the Shadows
programme.desc: The turtles spring into action to battle Shredder, mad scientist Baxter Stockman, Bebop, Rocksteady and the notorious Krang.

programme.title: The Wild Robot
programme.desc: Shipwrecked on a deserted island, a robot named Roz must learn to adapt to its new surroundings. Building relationships with the native animals, Roz soon develops a parental bond with an orphaned gosling.

programme.title: The Wiz
programme.desc: In this version of the classic story - based on the smash Broadway musical - Dorothy is a shy 24-year-old Harlem kindergarten teacher who is brought by a snowstorm to the mysterious Land of Oz, a sprawling, decaying, nightmarish, and dangerous megalopolis that resembles the physical characteristics of New York City.

programme.title: The Wiz
programme.desc: In this version of the classic story - based on the smash Broadway musical - Dorothy is a shy 24-year-old Harlem kindergarten teacher who is brought by a snowstorm to the mysterious Land of Oz, a sprawling, decaying, nightmarish, and dangerous megalopolis that resembles the physical characteristics of New York City.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Uncle Buck
programme.desc: A bungling bachelor becomes a last-minute babysitter. Buck's brother's three children are foisted on him when the parents are called away from home indefinitely. Although he seems to be an unsuitable guardian, Buck turns out to have an unexpected knack for parenting, but he must work hard to prove himself, especially when he needs to save his eldest niece from her unsavoury boyfriend.

programme.title: Trading Places
programme.desc: Sporting billionaire brothers reverse the roles of a Wall Street commodities broker and a street hustler.

programme.title: A League of Their Own
programme.desc: Based on a true story, this film, set during World War II, tells the story of the all-female baseball league that was started while the American men were away fighting in Europe. After a team of potential players has been recruited, former player Jimmy Dugan coaches the girls and prepares them for the World Series.

programme.title: Terms of Endearment
programme.desc: While widow Aurora fends off a stream of admirers, daughter Emma learns the hard facts of married life.

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption
programme.desc: Andy Dufresne and Ellis Boyd `Red' Redding are two prisoners serving long sentences in the vicious Shawshank jail. The two come to terms with their imprisonment while surviving the sadistic acts of prison warden Samuel Norton, and for both, a different sort of redemption awaits.

programme.title: Trading Places

programme.title: Planes, Trains and Automobiles
programme.desc: Fastidious businessman Neal Page and slobbish shower-curtain salesman Del Griffith are the reluctant odd couple trying to get home from New York to Chicago to be with their families at Thanksgiving whose thwarted travel plans keep throwing them together in.

programme.title: Caddyshack
programme.desc: A rich property developer wants to take over an exclusive country club and so challenges its pompous president to a game of golf leading to utter mayhem, and a stream of surreal and crude visual gags.

programme.title: Singin' in the Rain
programme.desc: When the transition is being made from silent films to `talkies', everyone has trouble adapting. Don and Lina have been cast repeatedly as a romantic couple, but when their latest film is remade into a musical, only Don has the voice for the new singing part. After a lot of practise with a diction coach, Lina still sounds terrible, and Kathy, a bright young aspiring actress, is hired to record over her voice.

programme.title: Blazing Saddles
programme.desc: Irreverent spoof about a black sheriff hired by a crooked speculator to drive citizens out of an all-white town which lies in the path of a railroad. The townspeople turn on the inexperienced sheriff, and he is forced to turn to a convict, once the fastest gun in the West, for help.

programme.title: Tootsie
programme.desc: An out-of-work actor dresses in drag for an audition, only to land the female lead in a TV soap. When he becomes the most popular character on the show, he feels compelled to carry the persona into his everyday life. While bringing him phenomenal success as an `actress', life as a woman also has its problems - especially when he falls for an actress with whom he works.

programme.desc: When Gabby's dollhouse, her most prized possession, ends up in the hands of an eccentric cat lady named Vera, she sets off on an adventure through the real world to get the Gabby Cats back together before it's too late.

programme.title: They Will Kill You
programme.desc: A woman answers a cryptic ad for a housekeeping job at a luxurious yet foreboding New York City high-rise. Upon arrival, she discovers residents have vanished without a trace for decades, fuelling whispers of a satanic cult lurking in the shadows.

programme.title: Fuze
programme.desc: London undergoes a citywide evacuation when a World War II bomb is unearthed at a construction site. Amid the escalating chaos, a daring criminal operation is set in motion -- one that uses the evacuation as a cover for a meticulously planned heist.

programme.title: Sisu: Road to Revenge
programme.desc: Korpi dismantles the house where his family was murdered and loads it on a truck to rebuild it somewhere safe. He soon finds himself in a violent cross-country chase as the Red Army commander who killed his family comes back to finish the job.

programme.desc: On August 7th 1974, at 7.15am, 24-year-old Frenchman Philippe Petit stepped off the edge of the South Tower of New York's World Trade Centre. Armed only with a balancing pole and intense determination, he walked out onto a single suspended wire and `danced' for 45 minutes while balancing a quarter of a mile above the pavement below. The is the intimate story of Petit and his group of friends as they followed their dream.

programme.title: WWII's Most Daring Raids

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain

programme.title: Clifford

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: When Hopparoo bursts all their bubbles with his sharp ears, the twins use McCoggins' extra-strong bubble mixture instead and blow a bubble so huge that it engulfs Battersby, Madame Au Lait, McCoggins, Lopsiloo, Chikidee and finally Tommy and Tallulah themselves! Where is Hopparoo? Only his sharp ears can free them for Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah love dressing up and surprising each other with their crazy costumes, but when they decide to surprise everybody else in town too Hopparoo is not impressed. In their efforts to surprise the bunny, they give the Spring Chicks such a fright that they barricade themselves in front of the clockhouse, blocking the way to Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Madame Au Lait is teaching the twins pottery, and to inspire them she shows them a magnificent cow pot that she made on her wheel, but when she leaves them to their enthusiastic pot-making, they accidentally smash it and when they try to repair it, they only make things worse. Trying to re-create the pot is even more of a disaster. Huge quantities of clay fly from their wheel into the town, blocking Pufferty's tracks.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Hopparoo is disappointed when everyone gathers for Measuring Time. He was sure he'd grown more than that! Tommy and Tallulah have some crazy ideas about how to add inches, but a super-tall rabbit rampaging through town on stilts can only cause chaos.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah are playing pirates. They are hunting for Madame Au Lait's treasure, but rival pirate Hopparoo keeps trying to commandeer their cardboard boat by telling them that it's Chime Time, causing them twice to abandon ship and run! So they don't believe him when Chime Time really comes round.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The twins are under strict instructions to keep clean for Madame Au Lait's town photograph, but this proves difficult when the rest of the town need their help with some messy jobs. So when it's Photo Time, they are covered in mud, compost and feathers.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Talluah are determined to throw a magnificent party for McCoggins' birthday. They get everyone involved and have fun organising the decorations, music and food, but Chikidee gets stuck in a giant cake and rolls around causing chaos.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah are trying to make everyone laugh, but McCoggins is not responding. The twins do everything they know to make him crack and eventually he laughs so loud that he gets a case of gigglitis, but McCoggins was working on a very delicate job at the clock and now they need to cure him in time for Chime Time.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Confuse-Us the Carp
programme.desc: It's a topsy-turvy day as Bao Bao the panda and Horace the hare set off on their morning walk - nothing is as it should be and the two friends can't understand why. Confuse-Us the wise old carp tells them that the ping and the pong are out of balance. The friends set off to try and put things right.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Bao Bao's Long Goodbye
programme.desc: Bao Bao the panda and Cassandra the crane are best friends. When Cassandra announces that autumn is almost here and she will soon leave, Bao Bao is upset at her imminent departure and prepares himself for life without her. Bao Bao learns that Cassandra is only leaving for the winter months, and he should make the most of her company while he can.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Savannah Craze
programme.desc: Doris the duck is fed up of always playing the same old games with her friends. She wants to invent a new and fun game they can all play but her other friends are reluctant to join in. She heads off on her own, and soon the other animals are intrigued by the strange bouncy boingy noises that can be heard across the savannah.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of a Long Night in the Jungle
programme.desc: Giggles and Tickles are so excited about their birthday the next day that they vow to stay up all night long so that they don't miss a single moment of it. The other animals on the savannah are tired and soon go to bed. Can the mischievous monkeys find ways to keep themselves awake all night and still enjoy their birthday?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of a the Record-Breaking Puffins
programme.desc: Gunnar the gull is encouraging the puffins to break as many records as they can. Their noise is distracting Hercule Moustache, who is busy trying to build a beach sculpture. The puffins do their best not to disturb the walrus.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Ronald and His Boulders
programme.desc: Ronald the rhino's favourite pastime is bashing boulders - but one day he realises there are no more boulders left to bash on the savannah. Ronald sets out to find something else to bash - with almost catastrophic consequences.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the House of Leaves
programme.desc: Nelson the elephant wants to build the biggest and best house of leaves ever. His nephew Nigel is eager to help out, but Nelson is worried that the leaf house might be too fragile for his exuberant young nephew. When the house of leaves comes crashing down, Nelson wastes no time in telling Nigel off.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Gertie the Goat
programme.desc: Gertie the goat has agreed to take Bao Bao the panda on a climbing expedition up the So Hi Mountains. The other animals advise Gertie against the trip, since pandas are not renowned for their climbing skills. Will Gertie and Bao Bao prove the others wrong, or will they need some help from their friends?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Jungle Ball
programme.desc: The Jungle Ball is imminent and all the animals are busy finding their dance partners for the night. All except Reginald - nobody has ever asked him to dance with them at the Jungle Ball. He sets off in search of a dance partner, but has he left it too late?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Great Rains
programme.desc: The savannah animals are busy preparing for the great rains, which are due any day. The rains flood the plains every year and they will have to take shelter on Blue Mountain. This year it is Giggles and Tickles' turn to keep watch for the start of the rain.

No one has seen it yet and the whole town is waiting for the grand unveiling, but when McCoggins asks Tommy and Tallulah to help him finish his model, the twins accidentally smash it.

programme.title: Songs From The Bright Side Specials

Songs From The Bright Side Specials
Songs From The Bright Side Specials
Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah have such fun with their new friend Larry the Bug that they decide to adopt him as a pet, but just as they finish making him a home, Larry flies away! After spending the day chasing him all over town, they are surprised to discover that Larry has a family of cute little baby bugs.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah enlist Battersby as their star performer for Show Time. Unfortunately, the magic trick they devise for him doesn't go to plan and soon Madame Au Lait is stuck down Hopparoo's portable hole. Tallulah's trumpet is also trapped and she needs it for Chime Time.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.desc: Nelson is in an inventive mood, and agrees to make various objects for his friends. Alan the aardvark is excited about the scooter that Nelson is making him - even though he doesn't really know what a scooter is. The other animals all take it in turns to help deliver the scooter to Alan, each making their own modifications to the vehicle on the way. Alan is delighted when his very own scooter eventually arrives.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Reginald's New Look
programme.desc: Nathalie the antelope decides to give Reginald the lion a makeover to refresh his look. She restyles him to such an extent that none of the other animals recognise him when he tries to alert them of an imminent danger. Will Reginald be able to lead his friends to safety in time?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.desc: It is Washi-Washi day in the So Hi Mountains, when all the animals wash and groom themselves until they are squeaky and clean. While all the other animals are having fun getting clean, Horace the hare keeps making excuses not to join in. Can Horace's friends persuade him that bathing can be great fun?

programme.title: Border Security: Canada's Front Line
programme.desc: An American forgets to fill out his forms before arriving at Customs with firearms. At Alberta customs, a traveller makes a breathtaking statement.

programme.title: Border Security: Canada's Front Line
programme.desc: An irritated traveller pushes officers to the limit, some fruit brought back from Haiti seems to contain much more than vitamins, and the story of a cabinet-maker who wants to go to dinner in British Columbia does not hold water.

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A blind busker is robbed of his day's takings, a hotel receptionist puts an armed robber in his place, and a helpful neighbour gives police a live commentary on a break-in.

programme.desc: A medical student turns detective to track down the driver who has left him for dead in a hit and run. Also a sweet-toothed burglar is tempted by a slab of fudge. And a wildlife camera catches some unusual nocturnal activity: burglars.

programme.desc: Saying goodbye to the tropics and dipping into cool southern-coast waters. Coming face-to-face with majestic grey nurse sharks, shape-shifting cuttlefish and huge blue whales, and playing with penguins and sea lions.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: Every empire needs a succession plan. Making babies - and raising them - is crucial for every species to survive. In some social circles, it takes a village to raise a family. In others, a single parent is all that stands between the vulnerability of youth and the threats of the wild.

programme.title: Animal Empires
programme.desc: Once-powerful animal empires like the dinosaurs have been completely wiped from the planet. But others once on the verge of extinction have survived, bounced back, and even reclaimed their kingdoms.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: The USA is big on flash and spectacle. Bighorn rams rattle the mountaintops, brown bears battle, desperate to fatten up for winter, sharp-tailed grouse shake their tail-feathers on the dance floor, and wild stallions rumble with beauty and grace.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: From the Highlands to the Alps, Europe's evocative landscapes are the backdrop for epic wildlife drama. Normally stoic red deer go wild during the rut, wolf packs clash over territory, and foxes do the foxtrot, but it's not as nice as it sounds.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: Wildebeest battle for herd hierarchy, floppy fish fight it out on land and locusts give up their vegetarian ways in a frenzy of cannibalism. It all happens on the wild and wide-open landscape of South Africa.

programme.title: Battle of the Alphas
programme.desc: The latitudes south of the Sahara offer vastly different battlegrounds for a wide range of warriors. Male giraffes swing their heads like wrecking balls, zebras boast the most powerful kick in the animal kingdom, and chimpanzee warfare is eerily familiar.

programme.title: Coral City
programme.desc: Adapt or perish in the coral streets as higher-than-normal temperatures make autumn cooling (and survival) more difficult. Shack up with a clownfish family and their sea anemone landlord, then see cleaner fish give manta rays a day at the spa.

programme.title: Coral City
programme.desc: Get chills as winter descends, slowing down life on the bustling reef. Meander with reef sharks and octopuses, visit a coral oasis atop an old shipwreck and more.

programme.title: High Life in the Dead Mountains
Wild Science
programme.desc: Using CGI, archive footage and science, this series reveals how creatures from deep seas to land predators hunt, survive and dominate nature.

programme.title: Life's Journey
programme.desc: The first few days of life are frightening.

programme.title: Life's Journey
programme.desc: From getting to grips with their bodies and learning to feed themselves, to practicing fighting skills and learning how to form friendships, young animals must learn life lessons in order to survive.

programme.title: Life's Journey
programme.desc: For every species on earth, mating is the purpose of life. But it's not easy, it can be a struggle to find a mate and a fight to gain access. Only the strongest and most determined will win at Luangwa's mating game.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: Using the latest audio technology, David Attenborough explores the crucial role of sound in nature, examining how it shapes and influences the everyday lives of animals, from communication to their behaviour and decisions.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: From melodic songs to strange hums, David Attenborough sets out to discover the sounds that gibbons, lyrebirds, bull elk, and more use to find a mate and warn off the competition.

programme.title: Secret World of Sound with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough follows baby animals as they communicate in their quest for survival, using sound to beg and call for help in the vulnerable early days of life in the natural world.

programme.title: Jaguar Journals
programme.desc: Lizzie gets to know Fera, the Queen of the Pantanal, as she teaches her rambunctious cub Simba some essential jaguar skills, including how to climb trees and hunt for dinner.

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: In some of the most-remote regions on the planet, researchers dive deep and brave danger to unlock secret shark behaviours, challenge myths about sharks, and fight for the protection of these supreme predators - before it's too late.

programme.desc: The Secret Life of the Hermit Crab chronicles the journey of a small community of Hermit crabs in Guadeloupe.

programme.title: Wild Pacific Rescue
programme.sub-title: Baby Sea Otter Finds a Family
programme.desc: The team in saves a massive sea lion caught in a plastic noose, a visit to the Rescue Centre, where a harbour seal pup may lose its eyesight and a baby sea otter needs a risky but life-saving surgery.

programme.title: Wild Pacific Rescue
programme.sub-title: Battle to Save a Sea Lion
programme.desc: The Rescue team tries to save an otter as Dr Marty battles rough conditions to help an entangled sea lion. At the Rescue Centre, a mischievous seal pup puts himself in danger.

programme.title: Wild Pacific Rescue
programme.desc: The MMR team helps a lost sea turtle get back home. A huge sea lion is found shot with a cross bow bolt, and Dr Marty helps a fruit bat cope with old age.

programme.title: Bugs That Rule the World
programme.desc: The ruthless realm of some of the stealthiest and cleverest insects in the animal kingdom -- from praying mantises and aquatic insects to dragonflies.

programme.title: Bugs That Rule the World
programme.desc: The crucial ecological roles that beetles play in nearly every environment, and why they are often regarded "the most important species on the planet."

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Descend into the tide pool, where a hermit crab house hunts, a baby catshark runs into strange neighbours, and an octopus terrorises prey. Watch seagulls and other creatures hunt on the surface as the heat rises and oxygen depletes in the pool.

programme.title: 13 Hours to Survive
programme.desc: Count down the seconds as the sun sets and predators prowl around the pool before the tide finally rolls in. Persevere as night falls with a kleptomaniacal slug and an acrobatic scallop, while a pair of sneaky crabs take a dangerous shot at romance.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Nearly every species on Earth must find a mate and while some become partners for life, for others it's just a fleeting moment.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: All airborne creatures embark on remarkable journeys through the air, some routes are short and others across hundreds of miles.

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: Get acquainted with an array of ocean oddballs and learn how billions of years' worth of changes, from ice ages to asteroid strikes, have shaped their looks and skills. Meet blind worms who fish, walking sharks, tree-climbing crabs, and much more.

programme.title: Alien Abyss

programme.title: Alien Abyss
programme.desc: Exploring the bizarre mating rituals of the ocean's most peculiar creatures. Learning how bloody battles for mates, gender bending, and super-size litters of babies might secure a next generation for these animals.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Wild Science uses CGI and research to reveal how animals survive, tracing their evolution and future across Earth's most extreme habitats.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough charts the story of animals who defy gravity, starting with the planet's first flyers: the insect.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies
programme.desc: After meeting some critters who glide through life in Borneo, Sir David Attenborough decides to turn back the clock.

programme.title: David Attenborough's Conquest of the Skies

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Drought
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered its winter season. Predator and prey are forced to live in close proximity due to a limited supply of water.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Flood
programme.desc: Waters flood Southern Florida's mangroves and marshes. Many of the 600 animal species that live in the Everglades disperse during this time, expanding their hunting and breeding grounds.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: A rugged, sky-high plain in the remote heart of Bolivia, the Andean Altiplano lays claim to towering snow-capped peaks, glowing multi-coloured lagoons, and the world's largest salt desert.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: A diverse trek through the remote and surprising canyon lands of southern Utah reveals soaring cliff walls, forested plateaus, and breathtaking gorges.

programme.title: Wild Wild East
programme.desc: A look at the relationships between species like lobsters, scallops and crabs which are are numerous and complicated. Changing water temperature is bringing invasive species to the Wild Wild East, putting pressure on species humans count on.

programme.desc: When their father passes away, his estate is left to his youngest son Bharat, which angers the eldest, who subsequently tries to kill him.

programme.desc: Drama about a psychiatric nurse who places her career in jeopardy when she falls in love with a patient.

programme.desc: The plot revolves around two young men, Ajay and Shlock born under challenging circumstances and their struggle and gain a decent life.

programme.title: Raaste Pyar Ke
programme.desc: Mohan falls in love with Gouri. Later, Gouri learns that her friend Shyama also loves Mohan and decides to sacrifice her love for Shyama.

programme.title: Do Aur Do Paanch
programme.desc: Two petty thieves become involved in a plot to kidnap a wealthy man's son.

programme.title: Yakeen
programme.desc: Nikhil hires a private detective to follow his wife, who he believes is having an affair with a famous singer.

programme.title: Palay Khan
programme.desc: The British have been asked to crush all opposition in India region by whatever means necessary. One of the strongest opposition to their rule is the indestructible Palay Khan.

programme.title: Safar
programme.desc: Medical students Avinash and Neela fall in love, but ashamed of his poor background, Avinash demands that Neela marry someone who can provide her with a comfortable lifestyle. Eventually Neela marries a rich businessman, but when he finds out that she is in love with Avinash, he attempts suicide, which subsequently puts Neela in jail.

programme.title: Conversation with Javed Akhtar 2
programme.desc: Lyricist, poet, screenwriter, Javed Akhtar along with film experts Nasreen Ali and Shabana Azmi. Together, they provide fascinating and personal insight into the new book, `Talking Life'.

programme.title: Lord Tramp
programme.desc: Hughie's life as a penniless tramp is about to change when he inherits a title and a massive estate, with servants and snooty neighbours.

programme.title: The Vise
programme.desc: Stars Sandra Dorne and Laurence Naismith. An eminent psychiatrist returns home early from a conference to find his wife with another man, leading him to seek revenge.

programme.title: Danger UXB
programme.desc: Hitler's latest novelty terrifies and paralyses the inhabitants of a country town. More dangerous exploits with the bomb disposal unit in post Blitz London.

programme.title: The Hi-Jackers
programme.desc: A lorry driver meets Shirley at a cafe and offers her a lift. His truck carrying valuable whiskey is later hijacked.

programme.title: Legend of the Hidden City
programme.desc: The Princess's most trusted friend remains under arrest. Nina grows frustrated as Dean becomes more withdrawn.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sir Lancelot
programme.desc: Queen Guinevere receives a priceless jewel from the Rajah of Kaipur when she visits Mortaise Castle, but the jewel is stolen during a fire.

programme.title: Batman
programme.desc: Battling their way out of Lord Ffogg's fog in the Londinium Tower, Batman and Alfred, in a cab, and Robin in the Batmobile, head for Ffogg's estate to rescue Batgirl.

programme.title: Hawkeye and the Last of the Mohicans
programme.desc: Ethan Allen and the Green Mountain Boys are at war with the ruthless tax collectors, a bunch of outlaws, more Robin Hood than the Dalton gang. When the authorities start to pin their misdeeds on them, Hawkeye must sort it out.

programme.title: Captain Scarlet and the Mysterons
programme.desc: Captain Black duplicates Major Reeves and engineers the launch of a Variable Geometry Rocket from Base Concord. Captain Scarlet and Captain Blue must stop the rocket before it is too late.

programme.title: The Adventures of Champion, The Wonder Horse
programme.desc: Sandy investigates Ricky's wild tale of a medicine show operator who supposedly stuffed a small boy into a box.

programme.title: The Buccaneers
programme.desc: Taffy backs out of marriage with one of the colony girls. In retaliation the girls threaten to leave. Tempest tries to broker a new proposal.

programme.title: The Adventures of Robin Hood
programme.desc: A beggar gets taken in by Robin and his friends after he gets accused of murdering the sheriff's brother.

programme.title: Target Earth
programme.desc: A large city is ordered to be completely evacuated as an army of robots, believed to be from the planet Venus, attack it.

programme.title: Look at Life
programme.desc: 1964. A look at how Malta has sought new methods of generating income after the demise of the great naval dockyard. Also, a look at the future of the island before its independence day in 1965.

programme.title: The Malta Story
programme.desc: A tribute to the people and defenders of the besieged island of Malta, centred on the pivotal role played by reconnaissance pilot Lt Peter Ross in World War II.

programme.title: Whiplash
programme.desc: Mowamba flats is the start of a new coach run for Cobb but some of the passengers do not appear to be what they claim.

programme.title: The Long Arm
programme.desc: A Scotland Yard detective is on the trail of a safe robber-turned-killer.

programme.title: Dick Turpin
programme.desc: When Dick thinks Swiftnick is failing to cut it as a highwayman, he bribes Tanner the gunsmith into taking the boy in as an apprentice.

programme.title: Maigret
programme.desc: Maigret is alarmed to learn that one of the most dangerous criminals in Europe is newly arrived in Paris.

programme.title: The Duchess of Duke Street
programme.desc: John Hawkesworth, having created 'Upstairs, Downstairs', went on to create this period drama. Louisa is curious when a young lady asks for Lord Haslemere.

programme.title: Honest, Decent and True
programme.desc: Trendy employees of a small London ad agency try to market beer for an old-fashioned client.

programme.title: Fortean TV

programme.title: One Step Beyond
programme.sub-title: The Dead Part of the House
programme.desc: A girl persuades her father to let her have a room of her own, not knowing the room's tragic history.

programme.title: Eurotrash

programme.title: Brides of Blood
programme.desc: Americans investigating the results of nuclear radiation discover a man-eating monster on a remote island.

programme.title: Look At Life
programme.desc: 1963. A Look into the trials and tribulations that confront drivers as parking is one of the major problems today in Britain's big cities.

programme.title: The Veil
programme.desc: An old farmer is dying while his two boys argue over his will, and a mysterious message points them to the story of Esau and Jacob in the Book of Genesis.

programme.title: A Little of What You Fancy
programme.desc: A loving tribute to British Music Hall with Lily Morris and Gus Elen. Featuring original archive film, rare prints, portraits and photographs, it traces what remains of the great music halls.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: As Christmas approaches, Leonard's handsome colleague starts dating Penny, and Sheldon's obsession with gift-giving etiquette torments his friends.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Kripke challenges them to a robot duel, Sheldon jumps at the idea. The guys are all excited about building their `killer' robot, named Monte, but the duel goes awry and Monte is the causality. Elsewhere, Penny finally tells Wolowitz what she thinks of his crude behaviour toward women and he is devastated, and Leonard convinces her to go and apologise.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon develops a scientific procedure for making friends when he discovers that Kripke has control of a new supercomputer at the university.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Penny runs into financial trouble, Sheldon gladly offers to lend her money. Penny is uncomfortable with getting a loan from him and begins to act out her frustration, which confuses Sheldon. Leonard soon finds out that her financial woes started when she lent money to her ex-boyfriend, Kurt, who has never paid her back. Without Penny's knowledge, Leonard decides to get the guys together to confront Kurt.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: A disastrous visit from Leonard's mother brings Leonard and Penny closer together.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: A game of paintball leads to a squabble when Penny accidentally shoots a paint gun at one of Sheldon's cushions, leaving an ugly stain that can't be removed and motivating Sheldon to seek revenge.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: On a train to San Francisco, Leonard, Sheldon, Wolowitz and Raj discover that beautiful sci-fi actress Summer Glau is on board.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny's home-based business selling hair accessories gets out of hand when an over-caffeinated Sheldon takes charge.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny develops a rivalry with a sexy new female neighbour who threatens to become the new Penny.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is devastated when the geeky sales clerk in a comic book store hits on Penny. Even worse, she doesn't mind! Leonard can't believe that Penny would date one of `them', unless it is him. Sheldon tries to scupper the budding romance by constantly challenging the clerk to engage in dialogue from Batman.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Koothrappali take a heartbroken Wolowitz to Las Vegas, leaving Sheldon locked out of his apartment and forced to bunk with Penny.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Brittney has hundreds of tiny bumps over her chest that have destroyed her confidence; John has a nose the size of a plum and leans on his local family clinic for help; Dr. Lee's friend Raquel drops in and brings out her wild side.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Tyler's hands are so dry, cracked and painful that he considers getting them amputated; Ebonee covers the tiny bumps around her eyes with sunglasses; Ralph feels like a cyst factory with painful cysts covering his back.

programme.title: Dr. Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Dr. Lee has a surprise for Paula, who has oozing, odoriferous and painful legs. Mike needs to get rid of the bump on his head before two major life events. Amanda feels inadequate for her husband because of the saggy growth on her back.

programme.title: Mike & Molly
programme.desc: Molly catches Carl coming out of Victoria's room late at night, with all three parties agreeing that they cannot tell Mike. Though things only get more complicated.

programme.title: Mike & Molly

programme.title: Young Sheldon

programme.title: My 600-Lb. Life

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress

programme.title: Say Yes to the Dress

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Things begin to look up for Leonard when Penny makes a surprising revelation on her second date with Stuart, while Wolowitz enlists his friends' help when he discovers a critical design flaw in his project for NASA.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny reconsider their feelings for one another, but is there a future for them when Leonard and his friends decide to spend the summer working at the North Pole?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The third series begins with Sheldon going back to Texas in disgrace when he learns that his friends tampered with his Arctic expedition data because his quest for a breakthrough was becoming unbearable to live with. His friends are forced to follow him. Meanwhile, Leonard is peeved that his attempts to woo Penny are being constantly interrupted but is cheered up when they finally sleep together.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Penny feel awkward around each other after their romantic liaison. They agree to be friends but both find it hard to fight their attraction to each other. Meanwhile, Sheldon and Howard make a bet to determine a species of cricket.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tries to mould Penny into his dream woman and rewards her with chocolate when she does things he approves of in the hope that she will keep doing them. Howard and Raj go to a goth nightclub. They meet two women and things are going well until the ladies take the friends to a tattoo shop.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Raj must find a job or he'll be sent back to India so Sheldon suggests that Raj work for him rather than with him and Raj begrudgingly accepts the offer. Later, Howard is lonely when Raj starts working with Sheldon and hangs out with Leonard and Penny who wish to be left alone.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon enters a competition but is annoyed when he finds out that `Star Trek' actor Wil Wheaton is participating because Wil cancelled his appearance at a sci-fi convention that Sheldon had attended as a child.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon teaches Leonard about football so that he can try to fit in with Penny's friends. During the game Leonard attempts to demonstrate what he has learned, but his explanations alienate him from the group.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon finds Leonard and Penny's arguments about Penny's male friend staying over `inconvenient' and reveals that his parents fought a lot when he was a child and he hates to hear people arguing.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Woolwitz is scared about committing to Bernadette and so avoids her. He realises he's made a mistake and to compensate, proposes to her. Meanwhile, Kripke plays a prank on Sheldon during an interview. Sheldon is hell-bent on revenge but unfortunately for him, the dean catches him out.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny feels left out when Bernadette shows an interest in science and asks Sheldon to teach her about science. Eventually, she surprises everyone with what she's learned. Meanwhile, Woolowitz is jealous when Bernadette asks Leonard about his work. Sensing his jealous streak, Bernadette threatens to break up with him.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is horrified when his mother Beverly turns up unannounced. His horror turns to anger when he learns that Sheldon knew about the visit and had found out about his parents' divorce before him. Later, Penny and Beverly go out drinking together and Beverly and Sheldon kiss.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Raj go to a university party, Leonard disapproves of Penny's belief in psychics and Raj lusts after a girl named Abby whom he meets at the party. Will he finally get the girl?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Sheldon's flat is burgled. Determined to out-wit any thieves, the gang work on creating a state-of-the-art security system to protect the apartment.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon has `theorist block' and is finding it difficult to muddle through a formula he's been working on. Taking inspiration from Albert Einstein, he takes a job at the Cheesecake Factory to try to kick start his creativity.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to Switzerland on Valentine's Day to see the world's largest particle accelerator. Sheldon is gutted when Leonard asks Penny to go with him, as a Valentine's Day treat, instead of him. Sheldon tries to coerce the couple into letting him take Penny's place, will they relent?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is summoned to court for driving through a red light while taking Penny to hospital in an earlier episode. He misses meeting Stan Lee, creator of `Spiderman', at the comic book store and riles the judge so much that he ends up in prison. Penny feels so guilty that Sheldon has missed out on the opportunity to meet Stan Lee that she finds out his home address and takes Sheldon there.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang squabble over a ring which they believe was used in `The Lord of the Rings'. Leonard wants to give it back to its original owner, Koothrappali wants to sell it so that he can buy a jet ski but Sheldon becomes obsessed with the ring and starts to turn into Gollum from The Lord of the Rings. Can the guys reach an agreement and will Sheldon return to normal?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon has been awarded a prestigious prize by the university but is scared of collecting the gong at a ceremony because he knows he'll have to give a speech. Penny tries to gee Sheldon up using rousing references to `X-Men' and Leonard and Koothrappali offer psychological guidance without much success. On the night, Sheldon's nerves get the better of him and he has a drink before going on stage.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard tells Penny that he loves her but doesn't get the response he would have liked. Elsewhere, the gang go bowling and get a surprise when Sheldon's enemy, `Star Trek' actor Wil Wheaton, is playing in the opposing team. Will Sheldon get revenge on Wil?

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James explores the wilder shores of motoring to discover what happened to the cars of the future that we were promised. From improbable steam cars and ludicrous jet turbines he reveals how the petrol engine and the power it gave us came to dominate the 20th century. He'll take to the waves in an amphibious car, risk his life at the wheel of a notorious electric scooter and take a hair raising trip in the fastest driverless car on earth.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts the quiz show in which contestants are rewarded more if their answers are `quite interesting'. This edition features Sean Lock, Jimmy Carr, Jo Brand and Alan Davies tackling the subject of danger.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts the quiz show in which contestants are rewarded more if their answers are `quite interesting'. This edition features Arthur Smith, Clive Anderson, Vic Reeves and Alan Davies tackling the subject of discoveries.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Stephen Fry hosts the quiz show in which contestants are rewarded more if their answers are `quite interesting'. This edition features Alan Davies, Liza Tarbuck, Jeremy Clarkson and Neil Mullarkey tackling the subject of dogs.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A family Christmas party turns sour when Lucy announces that she has got tickets to see Jason Donovan in panto. Lee loathes panto, especially given Jason Donovan was Lucy's teenage crush. That night, while Lee and Lucy turn their backs on each other in bed, Lee has an unsettling dream in which he is dressed as Buttons and his whole life has turned into a pantomime.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Wendy paints a portrait of Lee's late father, Frank, and gives it to Lee and Lucy as a present. She is expecting them to love it, but that would require the painting to be not just competent but good. Once it's hanging on their wall, Lee and Lucy must contrive to lose the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the stupid mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she reads it.

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Martin Lewis is host, with guest panellists comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Modern Life is Goodish
programme.desc: Impish original comedy from Dave Gorman, who muses out loud on life's little foibles - including light bulbs, gravy boats, dot-to-dot books and Lego.

programme.title: Comedians Giving Lectures
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe curates the show that puts a novel spin on academia. Sara is joined by comedians Toussaint Douglass, Elf Lyons, and Frankie Boyle as they compete to deliver the best lecture.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: A twist on the classic quiz show formula, contestants search through the depths of their knowledge in an attempt to score as few points as possible.

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Auction anarchy in Wales, where the bidders hope to get rich quick by bagging valuable storage lots. It's cloak-and-dagger stuff as spying equipment is on offer.

programme.title: Storage Hunters UK
programme.desc: Viewers of a sensitive disposition beware - one of today's lots has the bidders gagging! And why is Heavy D dressed as a ninja? The classic auction antics continue.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: James and Richard enjoy camping in the wettest part of the UK, using a variety of Cabriolets, while Jeremy tests the Alpha Romeo 147 GTA. Jodie Kidd is the guest.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson finds a novel way to drive the new Vauxhall Signum, and General Motors' revolutionary Hy-wire concept car is tested. `Star Trek' and `X-Men' star Patrick Stewart is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, plus a look at the Volkswagen Phaeton.

programme.title: Motorhoming With Merton & Webster
programme.desc: Paul Merton and his wife Suki Webster are joining the masses on the A-roads, embracing Britain's booming motorhoming scene, seeking out the UK's most famous sights and hidden gems.

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: Simon visits Brazil, the biggest country on the continent. He starts in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon, with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: aul and Bob return to Scotland to fish for salmon once more, this time on the mighty River Tweed. They start their search for a so-called bar of silver surrounded by banks of lush tall trees at the Bemersyde Estate, a place where it is easy to forget the outside world and do nothing but concentrate on fishing.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul takes Bob on a trip down memory lane and back to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to come as a teenager with his mates. They would catch the train from north London to fish this network of rivers and canals that flow from Hertfordshire into the Thames, and just like when Paul was young, Paul and Bob fish for perch.

programme.title: Mortimer and Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Bob and Paul revisit the River Wye in Herefordshire, this time they head to the spectacular Lower Wye to fish for chub. Bob has decided to meet Paul at the river and makes his entrance from upstream - on a rowing boat.

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The naturalist visits uncharted territory in pursuit of new discoveries. He takes on physical challenges, encounters extraordinary wildlife and meets remarkable people.

programme.title: Simon Reeve's South America
programme.desc: Simon visits Brazil, the biggest country on the continent. He starts in one of the remotest regions of the Amazon, with the Waiapi people, who cling on to their traditional way of life, which is under threat from logging and mining interests.

programme.title: Borderforce: America's Gatekeepers
programme.desc: Following the Department of Homeland Security Customs and Border Protection agency as they operate on the 28 bridges connecting the United States of America and Mexico.

programme.title: Special Ops: Crime Squad UK
programme.desc: Special police task forces tackle a brutal stabbing of a 16-year-old boy in Preston two days before Christmas, and Northamptonshire police target a drug-dealing gang on an estate.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: A family Christmas party turns sour when Lucy announces that she has got tickets to see Jason Donovan in panto. Lee loathes panto, especially given Jason Donovan was Lucy's teenage crush. That night, while Lee and Lucy turn their backs on each other in bed, Lee has an unsettling dream in which he is dressed as Buttons and his whole life has turned into a pantomime.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Wendy paints a portrait of Lee's late father, Frank, and gives it to Lee and Lucy as a present. She is expecting them to love it, but that would require the painting to be not just competent but good. Once it's hanging on their wall, Lee and Lucy must contrive to lose the painting without Wendy or Geoffrey noticing.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Lee accidentally sends a rude text about Anna to Lucy, he makes the stupid mistake of sending it to Anna herself. Lee and Lucy now face the impossible task of stealing Anna's phone and deleting the text before she reads it.

programme.desc: Martin Lewis is host, with guest panellists comedian Ignacio Lopez and broadcaster Steph McGovern joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: Lovejoy

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian Lane knows his days at UCOS are numbered. Meanwhile, the team investigate a 16-year-old murder of a young mum by her criminal husband that could be a miscarriage of justice. Gerry Standing feels very strongly that the conviction shouldn't be called into question.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: As Lane comes to terms with life after UCOS, Esther asks him to help one of her book club friends find her missing brother - but will his nose for the truth get him into further trouble? How will his former colleagues cope without their most dogged sleuth?

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: In the small, typically English village of St Mary Mead, the Bantrys of Gossington Hall are horrified to discover a female corpse in their library. Mrs Bantry immediately seeks help in solving the mystery from her friend and neighbour, Miss Marple, who follows clues which lead her to a seaside hotel and a second body.

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Frances is at a loss when she cares for diabetic and recovering cancer patient Albert Calthorpe whose capable wife Grace refuses to accept further help. programme.title: Catherine Cookson Dramas
programme.desc: Businessman Freddie Musgrave seems to have it all, until his shady past returns to haunt him.

programme.title: Home Fires
programme.desc: Drama series set in a Cheshire village during the Second World War. The isolated community could not feel further away from the impending bloodshed and battlefields, yet it is not immune from the effects of war, and everyone must make sacrifices as it begins to have an impact. Steph hides a secret that threatens the farm, Teresa's past comes back to haunt her and Erica is determined to intervene to protect Pat.

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: Michael's mother knows he can't live at home, but she wants to make sure he's all right. Hetty has to find him before its too late.

programme.title: Sister Boniface Mysteries
programme.desc: Peggy is delighted when old school friend and supermodel Robin returns home to film a documentary; all goes awry as Max Savage, Robin's manager, is found dead in his bedroom.

programme.title: Silent Witness

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallack's mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the village's mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand was one of the last land-masses to be found and settled by people. Lush and fertile, almost everything brought here flourishes, often with surprising consequences. Told through the experiences of its native species - in particular, a charismatic and peculiar giant, flightless parrot - this is the moving story of the changing fortunes of New Zealand's wildlife, since humans first arrived.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: As the urban world grows, animals are displaced from land that belonged to them. From herons to humpback whales, the stories of these wild outcasts are the most captivating of all.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: The small hamlet of Botallack was once at the centre of the Cornish tin and copper mining industry as archaeologist Ben Robinson discovers. By 1861, Botallack's mine was one of the biggest in the whole of Cornwall. Ben explores the village's mining past with archaeologist, Adam Sharpe, where he learns the mines produced millions and millions of pounds worth of copper and tin.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand was one of the last land-masses to be found and settled by people. Lush and fertile, almost everything brought here flourishes, often with surprising consequences. Told through the experiences of its native species - in particular, a charismatic and peculiar giant, flightless parrot - this is the moving story of the changing fortunes of New Zealand's wildlife, since humans first arrived.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: From pythons in Bangkok to otters in Singapore, a look at the extraordinary ways wildlife is making itself at home in the world's cities.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: Animals travel in and out of cities for food, shelter and to start a family.

programme.title: Wild Metropolis
programme.desc: As the urban world grows, animals are displaced from land that belonged to them. From herons to humpback whales, the stories of these wild outcasts are the most captivating of all.

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: When Reverend Pottle, the 102-year-old vicar of Dibley village dies, the locals are shocked to discover that his replacement is a woman. Geraldine invites all and sundry to call her Gerry, but the breath of fresh air she brings looks like turning into a foul wind as the chairman of the parish wants her removed.

programme.desc: The `Songs of Praise' team come to the parish to meet Geraldine. Unfortunately for her, the director of the team is rather dishy, while Alice continues to be devastatingly below par on the IQ front.

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers

programme.title: Great British Landmark Fixers

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The search goes on for the gunman who murdered Paul Sorrell after Devon Marshall and Jedda Atkins are released due to lack of evidence. Max is sure that local teenager Jasmine Harris has some vital information, but Mickey counsels against involving her further and is furious when Neil overrules him.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: An investigation is mounted when four-year-old Billy Sullivan goes missing from his father's garden. When CCTV footage comes to light showing Billy getting into a car, the officers start tracing the vehicle, but DS Stevie Moss and DC Jacob Banks soon discover that the number plates are false. They track the car to a local scrap yard and learn that the plates were sold to Peter Grigson, so the team set out to find him.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: The officers launch a murder investigation after a body is discovered following a crowd-control situation at a football match. When tensions rise between rival supporters, Supt Jack Meadows decides to take his officers to the ground to control and keep apart opposing fans. When the situation calms down, the officers begin a clear-up and come across a man's body, but it appears that he died in suspicious circumstances.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the football riot and the death of Yusef Hanoush, the team are under fire from the media, which speculates both that the police's `kettling' of fans contributed to Yusef's death, and also that the killing was race-related. Then CCTV evidence comes to light placing Commander Kennedy's son Mark at the scene of the stabbing.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Mickey is approached by Jimmy Ferrier, a known drug dealer, about the death of his brother Mitch. Mitch was an old friend of Mickey's and Jimmy hopes he can help. Mickey and Max attend Mitch's funeral and there become suspicious of Ryan Lacey - another drug dealer. Jimmy insists that Ryan is innocent but persuade Jimmy to arrange a meeting anyway.

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Jo and Benjamin get a call out to a hotel where Mark Pierce is demanding to be let in, claiming that his wife Julia is staying there. In her room, a man called Alan Marsh is lying badly injured while Julia has disappeared. She is later cleared when forensic results show that she is not the culprit, but how does Julia know the real attacker - Craig Chapman?

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: PCs Benjamin Gayle and Mel Ryder arrest Paul Rayner for assaulting a man he claims abused him as a boy, but it soon appears to be a case of mistaken identity, and when the team track down the man Rayner meant to target, they cannot charge him on Rayner's allegation alone. Rayner gives the police the name of another victim, but he refuses to testify, leaving Rayner devastated.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A woman fights for her life after being knifed by her former partner, while a teenager on her way to seeing two Youtube stars in a live appearance is felled by a falling tree.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: Go behind-the-scenes of HART, the Yorkshire Ambulance Service's 'Hazardous Area Response Team', one of 15 specialist medical units set up after the 7/7 London bombings to treat badly injured patients in extreme environments.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A teenager is found with multiple stab wounds in the street near his home, an amateur tree surgeon is badly hurt when a branch falls on his leg and two schoolgirls are run over.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team treat a lorry driver trapped behind his twisted steering wheel and help is urgently required for a 12-year-old who appears to be drunk after drinking an unknown substance.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg.

programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: First responders are called to help a patient having a massive heart attack; a car crash victim's lung may collapse; a cyclist gets winched to safety.

programme.title: First Responders
programme.desc: A routine winch job becomes a life and death situation on the Queen Charlotte Track; a rescue that the first responders will never forget.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When a historical documentary team comes to film at Button House, Alison and the ghosts are shocked to discover that the assassination plot they are investigating involved one of their own. Meanwhile, Mike, always on the lookout for free publicity, begs Alison to accept the offer of a television interview about the old house. The only hitch is that Alison is terrified of speaking on camera. Luckily, Thomas is there to coach her in the art of speechmaking.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is equally confused by his feelings for this strange visitor, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever over her surrogate sister Alison. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: While Button House is being fumigated for woodworm, Alison and Mike decide to save some money and camp in the grounds, sending the ghosts' routines off kilter.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Nan visits an old friend in hospital and leaves him a gift, Lauren takes a job at Billy's Burger Bar, and Geordie Georgie organises a fundraiser for battered husbands.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: 1950s landlady Ma Willow makes an appearance, the perpetually unbovvered Lauren competes with Kelly for Ryan's favour, and Nan has a mouse in the house. Special guests include Jools Holland and popular beat combo The Divine Comedy.

programme.title: The Catherine Tate Show
programme.desc: Bernie's family comes in for a visit, Derek Fay meets Bonnie Langford, and Lauren gets a surprising proposal.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Shana knows she can be uptight, but talking to friends about a terminal cancer diagnosis is daunting; the Death Cleaners help her face her fear of discussing "the end."

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: The untold true story of NFL superstar Aaron Hernandez from the viewpoint of those who knew him best strips away the layers to answer the many questions left behind after Hernandez's tragic death.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Janine and Adam assist a woman who is threatening to jump out of a first-floor window, and husband and wife team Ann and Kevin are called out to help Ann's sick Dad!

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Janey is appalled when her mother comes to visit her at university - especially when she begins to reminisce about her own student days. Meanwhile, Ben's plans for a relaxing round of golf do not go according to plan.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: Nick wins an old car in a poker game, and intends to break it up for parts. Ben takes it over and decides to restore it to its former glory, but he is about as good an engineer as Isambard Kingdom Brunel was a dentist.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: It's Ben's birthday, and everyone seems to have taken his request for no fuss literally, neglecting to buy him any presents. Janey is home for the weekend with a new friend, but nobody is sure of the nature of their relationship.

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: When the children go away for the weekend, Ben and Susan finally have some time to themselves. Unfortunately, it's been so long since that happened, that they have absolutely no idea how to spend it.

programme.title: My Family

programme.title: My Family

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: The Warden family bring in their one-year-old Saint Bernard, Poppie, who's suffering with hip dysplasia, and a 13-year-old collie called Tess is rushed to Fitzpatrick's after being hit by a car.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A four-year-old Chinchilla requires surgery for a broken leg, but his tiny airways and bones make it a difficult procedure. A puppy called Coco is rushed to the practice after being hit by a van, and Professor Fitzpatrick fights to save her limb.

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: The Supervet

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance

programme.title: Our Guy in China

programme.title: Our Guy in China
programme.desc: Guy Martin concludes his Chinese adventure with his toughest ever physical challenge. He wants to break the record for the fastest crossing of the Taklamakan Desert on a bicycle.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Following his Appleton's guide, Michael Portillo makes a trip to the jewel in the crown of America's National Parks, Yosemite, to behold its towering granite cliffs, its giant sequoia trees and cascading waterfalls. He discovers how early conservationists fought to protect it in law and encounters a `buffalo soldier' in the heart of the forest. In Berkeley, Michael uncovers a catastrophic event, which hit the city of San Francisco hard in 1906.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his late 19th-century Appleton's guidebook, Michael Portillo continues through southern California aboard the Coast Starlight rail service. In the historic port of Monterey, Michael raises the Bear Flag of the one-time Republic of California and above it, the Stars and Stripes. He discovers the city's Spanish and Mexican heritage and traces how the former fishing port described as `stinking and decrepit' in John Steinbeck's novel, `Cannery Row', has become a beacon for tourists.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo strikes oil in the suburbs of Los Angeles, contemplates his navel in the orange groves of Riverside, makes a California roll and paints a pretty picture in Laguna Beach. It is Mexican Independence Day and the locals are celebrating the country's hard-won independence from Spain in the early 19th century.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie navigates the wintery Grand Union Canal while preparing for Christmas and narrowly avoids flooding his boat in a tricky staircase lock.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: Simon Reeve travels from the beautiful Pacific coast of Mexico, where he visits the luxurious holiday resorts of Baja California, before crossing the country's rugged interior. The first stage of his journey ends in the Bahamas where he discovers the hidden menace on the beautiful coral reefs.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: The second leg of the journey sees Simon dodge the Moroccan secret police in Western Sahara, travel on one of the world's longest trains in Mauritania, visit a forgotten refugee camp in the Algerian desert where more than 100,000 people live, and take a swim in a Libyan oasis with a government minder who bears an uncanny resemblance to Colonel Gadaffi.

programme.title: Tropic of Cancer
programme.desc: Simon rejoins the Tropic in Egypt and follows it through Saudi Arabia, Dubai, and Oman. In Southern Egypt, next to the ancient temple of Abu Simbal, Simon meets Nubians struggling to maintain their culture following the damming of the Nile and dives in the pristine reefs of the Red Sea.
